Once upon a time, there was a huge bird. The bird was as big as a house. It had pretty colors on its wings. One day, the huge bird was flying high in the sky, looking for food. A little boy named Tim saw the huge bird. He thought it was very cool. He wanted to keep the bird and play with it. So, Tim found a big cage to put the bird in. He put food in the cage, hoping the bird would come. The huge bird saw the cage and the food. It went inside to eat. Tim closed the cage door and the bird was inside. The bird was sad, it wanted to fly free. Tim saw the bird was sad and knew it was not right to keep the bird in the cage. So, he opened the cage and let the bird go. The huge bird was very happy to be free again. It flew high in the sky and sang a happy song. Tim smiled and waved goodbye to the bird. He knew that the bird was happier flying free in the sky.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a funny cat named Tom. Tom loved to play with his toys. He had a toy with a long string that he liked to pull. Every day, Tom would pull the string and run around the house. One day, Tom found a bottle of ink in the kitchen. He did not know what it was, but he thought it looked fun to play with. So, Tom started to pull the bottle with his paws. The ink spilled on the floor and made a big mess. Tom's mom saw the mess and knew she had to clean it up. She was not mad at Tom, but she told him to be more careful next time. Tom learned that ink can make a mess, but he still had fun pulling his toy string.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, a little boy named Tim was playing in the park. He saw a big rock and wanted to climb it. But as he climbed, he began to feel scared. His legs shook and he stumbled back down. Tim's friend, Sue, saw him and said, "Don't be scared, Tim! I will help you." Sue found a big stick and said, "This is a shield. It will keep you safe." Tim smiled and took the shield. Together, they climbed the rock. When they reached the top, Tim and Sue looked around. They saw trees, flowers, and other kids playing. Tim was not scared anymore. He thanked Sue for her help and they played together until the sun went down.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom and Lily like to play police. They have hats and badges and a toy car. They pretend to solve problems and catch bad people. One day, they see a big mess in the park. Someone has broken the swings and the slide and the sandbox. Tom and Lily are sad and angry. They decide to solve the problem. They look for clues. They see footprints and candy wrappers and a broken bike. They follow the clues to a house. They knock on the door. A boy opens the door. He has chocolate on his face and a bandage on his knee. He looks scared. Tom and Lily ask him if he knows who made the mess in the park. He says he is sorry. He was bored and angry. He had a fight with his sister and ran away. He broke the bike and the swings and the slide and the sandbox. He says he will help clean up and say sorry to everyone. Tom and Lily are creative. They have an idea. They ask the boy if he wants to play police with them. They say they can be friends and have fun. They say they can help him with his sister and his bike. The boy smiles and says yes. He puts on a hat and a badge and gets in the toy car. They go back to the park. They clean up the mess. They say sorry to the people. They play on the swings and the slide and the sandbox. They have a good time. They solve the problem. They are good police. They are good friends.
Write a short story about Every.

Once upon a time, there was a successful hot dog stand run by a man and his wife. Every day people came to buy their delicious hot dogs. The man and his wife were always so happy and cheerful and it made everyone around them smile. One day, a stranger came to the stand and whispered to the couple, "I have a secret that will make you very successful." The couple was curious and asked the stranger to tell them what it was. The stranger said, "You must give away one of your hot dogs for free to everyone who comes to your stand." The couple was hesitant, but eventually decided to do as the stranger said. For the rest of the day, everyone who came to the stand was given a free hot dog. When the day was over, the couple noticed that they had made no money at all. They realized too late that the stranger had tricked them and that his secret was not successful at all. The couple had to close their stand and were very sad. The end.
Write a short story about little.

One day, a little red engine was going down the track. It was a fit engine, always happy and ready to work. The engine loved to help people and make them smile. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. It was a beautiful day. As the engine went down the track, it saw a big tree. The tree was in the way. The engine didn't know what to do. It thought very hard. Then, the engine had an idea. It took a deep breath and sneezed very loud. The sneeze was so strong that the tree moved out of the way. The engine was very happy and went on its way. But then, something unexpected happened. The engine saw a big pile of rocks on the track. The engine was scared, but it remembered how it moved the tree. The engine took a deep breath and sneezed again. The rocks moved, but not off the track. The sneeze made the rocks change into a big, friendly bear. The bear helped the engine move the rocks off the track, and they became good friends.
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a girl named Lucy. She loved to write with her soft crayons. One day, Lucy was writing on a big piece of paper when she saw a tiny bug. The bug was very quiet and did not make any noise. Lucy liked the silence and thought the bug was cute. Lucy said to the bug, "Hello, little bug! Do you want to be my friend?" The bug nodded its head and started to walk on the paper. Lucy picked up her crayon and began to write around the bug. She wrote the word "friend" next to the bug. The bug looked at the word and smiled. It then walked to the other side of the paper. Lucy wrote more words around the bug, like "happy" and "fun". The bug liked the words and danced on the paper. Lucy laughed and clapped her hands. Lucy and the bug played together and wrote words on the paper all day. They had lots of fun and became best friends. When it was time for bed, Lucy put the bug in a soft little bed she made. They both went to sleep, happy to have found a new friend.
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily and Max are playing with their toys in the living room. Lily has a doll and Max has a car. They are having fun, but they want to try something new. Lily suggests, "Let's switch toys. You can play with my doll and I can play with your car." Max agrees, "OK, let's switch. I want to see how your doll works." They swap their toys and start to play. Lily makes the car zoom and beep. Max makes the doll talk and dance. They are happy with their new toys. But then, Mom comes in and sees them. She is surprised and asks, "Why did you switch toys? Don't you like your own toys?" Lily and Max look at each other and smile. They say, "We like our own toys, but we also like to try new things. We switched toys because we wanted to have more fun." Mom nods and says, "I see. That's fine. As long as you are sharing and being nice, you can play with any toys you want." Lily and Max hug Mom and thank her. They continue to play with their switched toys, and sometimes they switch back. They are having a lot of fun.
Write a short story about Max.

Once upon a time, there was a charming little dog named Max. Max lived in a small house with a family. The family had a little girl named Lily. Max and Lily loved to play together. They played in the yard, and in the house. But there was one place they were not allowed to play - upstairs. One day, when the family went out, Max and Lily decided to play upstairs. They knew they were not allowed, but they wanted to see what was up there. As they played, a big toy box appeared in the room. Max and Lily were very excited and opened the box. Inside, they found many fun toys. But then, the toy box started to make loud noises. Max and Lily were scared. They knew they should not have played upstairs. They ran downstairs and hid under the table. When the family came home, they found Max and Lily hiding. The family told them it was okay, but they should always follow the rules. The moral of the story is to listen and follow the rules, as they are there to keep us safe.
Write a short story about Betty.

Once upon a time, there was a lively bird named Betty. She was always up for an adventure. One day, Betty got a wild idea. She wanted to try something totally new...she wanted to eat an oat! Cheerfully, Betty asked her friend, "Will you have an oat with me?" He looked back and replied, "Yes, why not?!" The two birds hopped over to a nearby oat patch and started to eat their fill. Betty was so excited, she chirped with joy. She had never tasted something so delicious before. But then, a terrible thing happened. Betty remembered that her little brother was allergic to oats! How could she have forgotten? Betty's friend stopped eating, shocked. In a gentle voice he asked, "Is your brother okay?" Betty shook her head sadly and replied, "I don't know yet. I hope he is ok." But the lively bird was determined to take care of her brother. Without wasting any time, she flew off to find him. When Betty found her brother, she was relieved to see that he had not eaten any oats. He told her what happened: he had felt something funny and itchy in his throat when they were talking about oats. So, he had run away, just in time. Betty hugged her brother. She was so thankful that he had not been hurt.
Write a short story about Spot.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to move and play all day. One day, Spot was very hungry. He wanted to find some food to eat. Spot went outside and saw a big tree. At the top of the tree, there was a point. Spot looked up and saw a yummy apple at the point. He wanted to eat the apple, but it was too high for him to reach. Spot had an idea. He moved a box under the tree. He jumped on the box and then jumped up to the point. Spot got the apple and ate it. He was not hungry anymore. Spot was happy and went back to playing with his friends.
Write a short story about Sam.

One day, a pale cat named Sam went for a walk. He saw a big tree and thought it would be a nice place to rest. As he lay under the tree, a little bird flew down and sat on a branch. "Hi, Sam!" said the bird. "What are you doing?" Sam replied, "I'm resting. I'm very tired." As they talked, Sam noticed a long stick on the ground. He thought it would be fun to measure how tall the tree was. He stood up and started to measure the tree with the stick. But suddenly, the stick moved! To Sam's surprise, the stick was not a stick at all. It was a long, thin snake! The snake looked at Sam and said, "Hello, I'm not a stick. I'm a snake!" Sam and the bird laughed. They had found a new friend in the snake, and they all spent the day playing together under the big tree.
Write a short story about Every.

Once upon a time, in a lively little town, there was a big clock. This clock was very important to the town. Every day, the people looked at the clock to know when to wake up, eat, and go to bed. One day, the clock stopped working. The people in the town did not know what to do. They needed the clock to tell time. So, a kind man named Tom decided to fix the clock. He worked very hard to set the clock right. When Tom fixed the clock, everybody was happy. The lively town was back to normal. The people woke up, ate, and went to bed at the right time. They thanked Tom for setting the clock and making their town lively again. And they all lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Hello.

Once upon a time, there was a little train. The train loved to go to the station. At the station, the train would meet many friends. They would talk and laugh all day long. One day, the train saw a sad little boy at the station. The boy did not touch or play with the other trains. The train wanted to help the sad boy feel better. So, the train went up to the boy and said, "Hello, I am a train. Do you want to play with me?" The sad little boy looked at the train and smiled. He touched the train and started to play. Please, do not be mad at me." Tom said. But Sara did not listen. She ran away with the doll. She did not want to play with Tom anymore. Tom felt sad and lonely. He did not understand why Sara was so upset. He did not know that the doll had a trick button. The trick button made the doll say a mean message. It was a joke. But it was not a funny joke. It was a bad joke. It hurt Sara and Tom's feelings. It made them lose their friendship. It was a foolish doll.

Write a short story about Sam.

Sam was a mule who lived on a farm. He liked to eat grass and hay, but he did not like to work. He was lazy and stubborn, and he always said no to the farmer. The farmer was angry with Sam, and he did not give him any treats or hugs. One day, the farmer had to take some apples to the market. He asked Sam to help him pull the cart, but Sam said no. He wanted to stay in his barn and eat more hay. The farmer was very angry, and he said, "Fine, you can stay here, but you will get no apples or carrots or anything else. You will only eat hay and water, and you will be alone and gloomy." The farmer left with another mule, who was nice and helpful. Sam did not care. He thought he was smart and happy. He ate some hay and drank some water, and then he took a nap. He did not notice that the sky was getting dark and cloudy, and that a storm was coming. Soon, it started to rain and thunder and lightning. Sam woke up and felt scared. He did not like the loud noises and the wet wind. He wished he had gone with the farmer and the other mule. He wished he had some apples or carrots or something else to eat. He wished he had someone to hug and talk to. He felt very gloomy and lonely. He looked out of his barn and saw a little girl walking by. She had a red coat and a yellow hat, and she was holding a basket. She saw Sam and smiled. She said, "Hello, mule. You look sad. Do you want to be my friend?" Sam nodded and said yes. He wanted to be her friend. The girl came closer and opened her basket. She said, "I have some cookies and milk for you. Do you want to eat them?" Sam nodded and said yes. He wanted to eat them. The girl gave him some cookies and milk, and then she hugged him and said, "You are a nice mule. I like you. Do you want to come with me to my house?" Sam nodded and said yes. He wanted to go with her to her house. He felt happy and warm. The girl and Sam walked away from the barn, and the rain stopped. The sun came out and made a rainbow. Sam looked at the rainbow and smiled. He was glad he had met the girl. He was glad he had eaten the cookies and milk. He was not gloomy anymore. He was a happy mule.

You have to stop singing and dancing right now. You have to clean your room and say sorry. And you have to punish yourself. You have to turn off your TV and your internet for one hour. You cannot watch your friends or your fans. You have to learn your lesson." Tom feels very sad and sorry. He knows he did a bad thing. He says, "Mom, I am sorry. I was bored and I wanted to have fun. I did not think you would hear me. I did not mean to make you angry. I will stop singing and dancing. I will clean my room and say sorry. And I will punish myself. I will turn off my TV and my internet for one hour. I will not watch my friends or my fans. I will learn my lesson." Tom's mom hugs him and says, "I love you, Tom. You are a famous cat, but you are also my son. You have to listen to me and obey me. You have to be good and quiet when I need you to be. You have to respect me and my work. You have to understand that singing and dancing are not the only things in life. You have to balance your fun and your responsibility. You have to grow up and be a smart cat." Tom nods and says, "I love you, Mom. You are my mom, but you are also a busy person. You have to work on your computer and do important things. You need silence and peace. I have to listen to you and obey you. I have to be good and quiet when you need me to be. I have to respect you and your work. I have to understand that working and helping are also things in life. I have to balance my fun and my responsibility. I have to grow up and be a smart cat."
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a boy named Tim went to his attic. He saw a big box. Tim opened the box and found a sour apple. He took a bite and made a funny face. The apple was not good to eat. Tim heard a small voice say, "Thank you!" He looked around and saw a tiny mouse. The mouse was happy because Tim had found her apple. But Tim did not like the sour apple. He wanted a sweet one. The mouse had an idea. She took Tim to her secret stash of sweet apples. Tim was surprised and happy. He thanked the mouse and they became good friends. They shared the apples and had a fun day together.
Write a short story about Everywhere.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy monkey who liked to climb. Everywhere he went, he was grumpy. One day, he decided to climb a tall wood. He climbed and climbed and climbed, until he was almost at the top. But then, he spotted something. Another monkey was stuck at the highest point of the wood. The grumpy monkey quickly climbed the rest of the way and reached the other monkey. He asked why the monkey was there, but the other monkey just shrugged. The grumpy monkey figured out that the other monkey had been too grumpy to ask for help and had gotten stuck. So the grumpy monkey helped his new friend, and they climbed down together. As they were climbing, the grumpy monkey said, "It's important to be nice and ask for help when you need it - even if you don't want to!" The other monkey smiled and agreed. When they reached the ground, the grumpy monkey went back to his own monkey life, no longer grumpy. He reminded himself that it's important to be kind and to be ready to ask for help - even if you don't want to.
Write a short story about Emma.

Once upon a time, there were two friends, Emma and Jack. They liked to play together. One day, they decided to create some stuff. They made some silly things with some paper and some markers. They made a paper wolf and a rainbow hat. They were so silly and had so much fun. Then they decided to make a castle from some blocks. They stacked the blocks carefully and put some bright colors on them. Soon, the castle was finished and it looked silly but very special. They were so proud of their creation. Then, Jack got an idea. He suggested that they draw a dragon on the castle. Emma was very excited and together they added a big purple dragon with golden eyes to the castle. It made them laugh and smile. The castle now looked even sillier, but they loved it. The two friends had so much fun creating silly stuff together. They had so many stories to tell with their creations!
Write a short story about Ben.

Ben and Lily like to play in the sand. They have a big bucket and a small shovel. They make hills and holes and castles. Sometimes they find shells and rocks and sticks. One day, they find a band in the sand. It is shiny and round and has letters on it. Ben says it is a ring. Lily says it is a bracelet. They do not know who it belongs to. They think it is a treasure. They decide to bury the band in the sand. They dig a deep hole and put the band inside. They cover it with sand and pat it down. They make a big X with sticks to mark the spot. They say they will come back later and dig it up. But then the sun gets very hot and the sand gets very dry. Ben and Lily feel thirsty and tired. They want to go home and have a drink and a nap. They forget about the band in the sand. They leave their bucket and shovel and run to their mom. The band stays in the sand. It is shiny and round and has letters on it. It is a treasure. But no one knows where it is. Maybe someone will find it someday. Or maybe it will stay buried forever.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big, soft carpet in his room. He loved to play on the carpet with his toys. One day, Tim thought it would be fun to invite his friend, Sam, to play on the carpet with him. "Sam, do you want to come play on my carpet?" Tim asked. Sam smiled and said, "Yes, I would love to play on your carpet with you, Tim!" So, Sam came over to Tim's house, and they had a great time playing on the carpet together. But then, they found a difficult puzzle. Tim and Sam tried to put the puzzle together, but it was hard. They did not give up. They worked together and finally finished the puzzle. Tim and Sam were happy they did it. They had so much fun on the carpet that day, and they promised to play together again soon.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a thoughtful girl named Lily. She loved to help her mom and dad. Lily had a special job. She had to complete a big patch for her mom's shirt. Lily went to her room and found her box of colors. She picked the best ones to make the patch pretty. She worked very hard to complete the patch. She wanted her mom to be happy. When Lily was done, she showed the patch to her mom. Her mom was so happy and gave her a big hug. Lily felt good because she was thoughtful and helped her mom. They put the patch on the shirt, and it looked great.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a big farm. On the farm, there were many animals. They all lived together in a big barn. The barn was very comfortable. The animals were happy. One day, the farmer heard a loud noise. The noise came from the barn. The farmer went to see what was happening. He saw that the animals were fighting. The farmer knew he had to separate the animals. The farmer made a plan. He put the chickens in one part of the barn and the pigs in another part. The noise stopped. The animals were happy again. They learned that sometimes it is good to be separate. And they all lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a little frog named Tim was hopping around the pond. He was a confused frog because he did not know how to pay for his new toy. He wanted to play with it so much, but he had no money. Tim met his friend, the big duck named Dan. "I need help," said Tim. "I want a new toy, but I don't know how to pay for it." Dan thought for a moment and said, "Let's find a way to get some money for your toy." They looked around and found some pretty rocks. They decided to sell the rocks to their friends. Tim and Dan worked together to sell the rocks. Soon, they had enough money for Tim's new toy. Tim was so happy when he paid for his toy. He thanked Dan for helping him. Now, Tim was not a confused frog anymore. He learned how to pay for things, and he played with his new toy all day.
Write a short story about Sue.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. She had a pink dress that she loved very much. One day, Sue saw a shiny gold rock in the park. She picked it up and took it home. Sue showed her mom the gold rock. Her mom said, "Sue, that rock belongs to someone else. We must give it back." Sue did not want to give the rock back, but she knew it was the right thing to do. So, they went back to the park to find the owner. At the park, they found a sad boy named Tom. He was looking for his gold rock. Sue gave the rock back to Tom. He was very happy and said, "Thank you, Sue!" Sue felt good about giving the rock back. Tom and Sue became friends and played together. They learned to forgive and share, and they lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, there was a bright red umbrella. It was very happy when it could help people stay dry when it rained. One day, the sun was shining, and the umbrella was sad because it could not help anyone. A little girl named Lucy saw the umbrella and had an idea. She wanted to cook outside with her toy pots and pans. She thought the umbrella could give her shade from the sun. Lucy opened the umbrella and started to cook her pretend food. But then, a big wind came and took the umbrella away. Lucy tried to catch it, but it was too fast. The umbrella got stuck in a tree, and Lucy was very sad. She learned that umbrellas are only for rain, not for cooking in the sun.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, in a small park, there was a swing. A little boy named Tim loved to play on the swing every day. One day, while Tim was swinging, he saw a cord hanging from a tree. He wanted to pull it but didn't know what would happen. A girl named Sue came to the park and saw Tim. She asked, "What are you doing?" Tim said, "I want to pull the cord, but I don't know what will happen." Sue thought for a moment and said, "Let's pull it together and see." So, Tim and Sue pulled the cord together. Suddenly, a big pinata appeared in the air! They were excited and started to hit the pinata. But then, a rude boy came and tried to take the pinata for himself. Tim and Sue didn't like that. Tim and Sue said, "No, you can't take it! We found it together." The rude boy didn't listen and tried to take it again. But then, a big wind came and blew the pinata away. The rude boy was sad, but Tim and Sue went back to play on the swing, happy to have had fun together.
Write a short story about Lena.

Lena and Tom are friends. They like to play in the park. They see a big door. The door is red and shiny. It has a sign that says "Do not enter". "Let's go inside!" Tom says. He is lively and curious. He wants to see what is behind the door. "No, Tom, we can't!" Lena says. She is careful and scared. She does not want to break the rule. "The sign says no. It is bad to go inside." But Tom does not listen. He urges Lena to follow him. He pulls her hand and runs to the door. He pushes the door. It is not locked. It opens with a loud creak. "Wow, look!" Tom says. He sees a lot of toys and games. He sees a train, a doll, a ball, a puzzle, and more. He is happy and excited. He wants to play with everything. "Tom, we have to go!" Lena says. She sees a man. The man is angry and stern. He is the owner of the toys and games. He does not like children to touch his things. He shouts at Tom and Lena. "Hey, you two! What are you doing here? This is my room! Get out of here, now!" Tom and Lena are afraid and sorry. They drop the toys and games. They run to the door. They close the door behind them. They run back to the park. "Tom, that was bad!" Lena says. She is sad and mad. She does not want to be Tom's friend anymore. "Sorry, Lena," Tom says. He is ashamed and sorry. He wants to be Lena's friend again. "I was wrong. I should have listened to you. Can we still be friends?" Lena thinks for a moment. She sees that Tom is sincere and sorry. She decides to forgive him. She nods and smiles. "Okay, Tom," Lena says. "We can still be friends. But next time, do not urge me to do bad things. And do not go inside doors that say no. It is dangerous and rude." Tom agrees and hugs Lena. They promise to be good and careful. They go to play in the park again. They have fun and learn. They are friends.
Write a short story about Stop.

One day, a cat and a dog were playing near a big water bowl. They liked to splash and scatter the water all over the floor. The cat would use her paw to scatter the water and the dog would wag his tail to do the same. "Stop!" said the cat. "I don't want to play this game anymore. We will get in trouble." The dog did not listen and kept on scattering the water. The cat felt guilty and walked away. Soon, the floor was all wet and slippery. The dog slipped and fell. "Ouch!" he cried. The cat came back to help the dog stand up. They both knew they had made a mess, and they felt guilty. They decided to clean up the water together and promised not to scatter water again.
Write a short story about Susie.

One sunny day, Susie and her dad were outside in the garden. Susie was looking for bugs to catch, and her dad said he was looking for something special. "What kind of something special, Daddy?" asked Susie. "A trap, Susie," he said. "A special trap that I can use to catch something really big." Just then, Susie saw something ordinary in the grass. It was a trap! "Here it is!" shouted Susie. Her dad was surprised. "Good job, Susie!" he said. He thanked Susie for finding the trap. Susie and her dad set it up and waited, but nothing happened. After a while, Susie got bored. "Let's go play," she said. Her dad agreed, and they left the trap behind, but they knew they could check it later. The end.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a sweet little girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom, dad, and her dog, Max. They all loved to play outside in their garden. One sunny day, Lily and Max were playing near the flowers, when they saw a broken hose. Lily wanted to fix the hose so they could water the flowers. She asked her dad for help. He showed her how to put the two ends of the hose together and wrap a special tape around it. Lily worked very hard and soon, the hose was fixed! After fixing the hose, Lily and Max took turns watering the flowers. The flowers grew big and colorful, making their garden even more beautiful. Lily felt happy and proud that she had fixed the hose with her dad's help. From that day on, Lily and Max always took care of their garden and their flowers.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a toy robot with a useful arm. He loved to play with his robot all day long. One day, Tim wanted to test his robot's arm to see how strong it was. So, he asked the robot to pick up a big ball. The robot tried and tried, but it could not pick up the ball. Tim was sad. Just then, a tiny ant walked by and saw Tim and the robot. The ant said, "I can help you!" The ant walked up to the ball and, with a big push, the ball moved! Tim was so surprised. The ant was so small, but it was very strong. Tim learned that even small things can be useful and strong. And they all played happily together.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom and Lily are playing with blocks. They have many blocks of different shapes and colors. They like to build towers and houses with them. "Look, I made a big tower!" Tom says. He shows Lily his tower of red and blue blocks. It is very tall and has a circle on top. "Wow, that is nice!" Lily says. She likes Tom's tower, but she wants to make something different. She has a yellow and green block in her hand. It is not a circle, it is a square. "I want to put this on your tower," Lily says. She tries to put the square on the circle, but it does not fit. It falls down and makes a loud noise. "Hey, don't do that!" Tom says. He is angry. He does not want Lily to ruin his tower. He pushes Lily away and grabs the square. "No, this is mine!" Lily says. She is sad. She wants to play with Tom, but he does not share. She pulls the square and tries to take it back. They both pull and tug on the block. They do not see that their tower is shaking. It is about to fall. "Stop, stop!" a voice says. It is Mom. She sees the children fighting and the tower wobbling. She runs to them and catches the tower before it crashes. "Tom, Lily, what are you doing?" Mom says. She is not happy. She does not like to see them fight. She puts the tower on the floor and looks at them. "Sorry, Mom," Tom and Lily say. They feel bad. They know they were wrong. "Can you say sorry to each other?" Mom says. She wants them to be nice and make up. "Sorry, Tom," Lily says. She gives him the square. She does not need it. She can find another block. "Sorry, Lily," Tom says. He gives her the circle. He does not mind. He can use another block. They hug and smile. They are friends again. "Good, now can you play nicely?" Mom says. She is glad. She likes to see them happy. "Yes, Mom," Tom and Lily say. They nod and look at their blocks. They have an idea. They can make something together. They can use the circle and the square and the other blocks. They can make an open house. They can put the circle on the door and the square on the window. They can invite Mom to see their house. They start to build. They are having fun. They are playing with blocks.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. He liked to play with his toy car. Tim had a new friend named Sam. Tim wanted to introduce Sam to his mom. They went to Tim's house to meet her. Tim's mom was in the kitchen. She was making cocoa for Tim and Sam. The cocoa was hot and smelled good. Tim and Sam sat down at the table. They were calm and happy to drink the cocoa. But, Tim was too excited. He forgot the cocoa was hot. He drank it too fast and burned his mouth. Tim cried and his mom felt sad. Sam was sad too. The day did not end well for Tim.
Write a short story about Spot.

Once upon a time, in a cool wood, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot loved to play with his ball. One day, Spot saw a big tree with a hole in it. He thought, "I wonder what is inside?" The next day, Spot went to the cool wood to play with his ball. He threw the ball high in the air, but he did not see where it went. Spot looked and looked, but he could not find his ball. He started to miss his ball very much. Spot remembered the big tree with the hole. He thought, "Maybe my ball is in there!" He ran to the tree and looked inside. There was his ball! Spot was so happy to find his ball. He played with it all day long in the cool wood.
Write a short story about Bob.

Once upon a time, there was a smelly dog named Bob. He loved to play with his friends in the park. One day, Bob saw a shiny spoon on the ground. He thought it was a fun toy, so he took it. Bob did not know that taking the spoon was wrong. He did not know that he should not steal. His friend, a wise cat named Sam, saw what Bob did. Sam went to Bob and said, "You should not take things that are not yours." Bob felt bad for taking the spoon. He wanted to make it right. So, he went back to the park and put the spoon where he found it. Bob learned not to steal and to be a good friend. From that day on, Bob and Sam played happily in the park, and Bob never took things that were not his.
Write a short story about Watch.

Once upon a time there were two children, a brother and a sister, out kayaking together on a quiet lake. "Watch out!" shouted the brother, as his kayak bumped against the sister's. Suddenly, a loud noise filled the air, and everything around them seemed to freeze. The brother and sister couldn't move. "What's happening?" asked the sister. The brother squinted in the direction of the noise. "There's a big bird over there," he said. They looked up and saw a huge, white bird hovering in the sky, looking down at them. The bird flew away and the spell was broken. The brother and sister continued paddling on the quiet lake, and all was as it should be.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a small girl named Lily. She had a key that could open any door. One day, she went for a walk in the woods with her key. It was a slow walk because she was looking at all the pretty flowers and trees. Lily found a door in the woods. She said, "I will use my key to open this door!" She put the key in the door and turned it. The door opened, and Lily saw a room full of ice cream. She was very happy and started to eat the ice cream. But as Lily ate the ice cream, it started to melt. The room became very wet and slippery. Lily tried to walk out, but she slipped and fell. "Help me!" she cried. But no one was there to help her. The ice cream melted more and more, and Lily could not get out. The door closed, and Lily was stuck in the room forever.
Write a short story about Jane.

Once upon a time, there were two friends called Jane and Jack. They went to a fancy hall to go and play. Inside, they laughed and joked until they felt so happy! When they looked around they saw a funny toy that made silly noises when they held it. Jane and Jack laughed as they pulled funny faces at it. Then they started to play hide and seek. Jane ran and hid behind one of the chairs in the hall and Jack went to find her. When Jack found Jane he thought it was very funny and he started to joke about it. Jane laughed and the two friends wanted to stay and play at the fancy hall forever. They laughed and joked until their tummies were filled with happiness. The end!
Write a short story about Apples.

Once upon a time, there was a big red apple tree. The tree had many apples. There was a stand under the tree. The stand had a sign that said, "Apples for All." One day, a little boy named Tim came to the stand. He saw two apples on the stand. One apple was big and shiny, and the other apple was small and not shiny. Tim wanted the big apple. He took the big apple and left the small apple alone. The small apple felt guilty. Tim started to walk away with the big apple. But then, he stopped. He looked back at the small apple. Tim felt bad for the small apple. He went back to the stand and put the big apple next to the small apple. He did not want to separate them. Tim said, "I will not take just one apple. I will take both apples and share with my friend." Tim took both apples and went to find his friend. The apples were happy to be together, and the stand was empty, waiting for more apples to come.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, in a big forest, there was a mighty rhinoceros. He loved to dance all day long. He danced in the sun, and he danced in the rain. His dance moves made all the animals happy. One day, a little bird came to the rhinoceros. The bird was sad because he could not dance. The mighty rhinoceros wanted to help the little bird. So, he taught the bird how to dance. They danced together, and the bird was happy. The other animals saw the mighty rhinoceros and the little bird dance together. They learned that helping others can make everyone happy. The animals in the forest danced together every day, and they all lived happily ever after. The moral of the story is to always help others, and happiness will come your way.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a blue ball that he loved to play with. One day, he went to the park with his mom to play with his blue ball. Tim was very happy. While playing, Tim saw an unknown dog. The dog was big and wanted to play too. The dog took Tim's blue ball in its mouth and ran away. Tim was sad and didn't know what to do. He wanted his blue ball back. Tim's mom saw him and asked what was wrong. Tim told her about the dog and his blue ball. His mom said, "Don't worry, we will get it back." They went to the dog and Tim's mom screamed, "No, bad dog!" The dog dropped the blue ball and ran away. Tim got his blue ball back and was happy again. They went home and played together.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a little boy named Tim went to play outside. He had a spade, a bucket, and a big smile. He wanted to fill his bucket with sand to make a sandcastle. The sun was shining, and it was a regular day at the park. Tim was very happy. Tim saw a girl named Lily playing near him. She also had a spade and a bucket. Tim said, "Hi, I am Tim. Do you want to play with me?" Lily smiled and said, "Yes, I am Lily. Let's make a big sandcastle together!" They both started to fill their buckets with sand using their spades. Tim and Lily worked together to make the biggest sandcastle in the park. They filled their buckets with sand and used their spades to pat it down. The other kids saw their sandcastle and clapped for them. Tim and Lily were so proud of their work. They became good friends and played together every regular day.
Write a short story about Max.

Once upon a time, there was a hairy dog named Max. Max loved to play with his ball. One day, Max saw a bottle of perfume on the table. He thought it was his ball. Max said, "I want to play with my ball!" He jumped up and tried to grab the perfume. But it was too high for him. Max asked his friend, a little bird, to help him. The little bird said, "Okay, Max! I will help you!" The bird flew up and grabbed the perfume with its beak. It dropped the perfume on the ground, and Max sniffed it. He realized it was not his ball after all. Max and the little bird laughed. They went outside and played with Max's real ball. They had a fun day together.
Write a short story about Hello.

Once, there was a parrot who loved flying around the neighbourhood. One day, he saw a rough looking man walking along the path. The parrot recognized him from across the street and swooped down to say hello. "Hello!" he yelled gleefully. The man stopped and looked at him. "Ugh," he said, "Get away from me, bird." The parrot flew away, feeling sad and rejected. He wanted to make friends but it seemed like the rough man didn't want that. The next day, the parrot spotted the same man walking down the street again. He flew over, determined to make friends this time. He was surprised to see the man pick up a rock and throw it at him. The rock hit the parrot and he fell to the ground. He didn't move. The man turned and walked away, not recognizing what he had just done. The parrot never flew again. He lay on the path, lifeless, with no one to recognize him.
Write a short story about Kitty.

Once upon a time, there was a charming cat named Kitty. Kitty loved to stretch and play all day. One day, Kitty met a big dog named Max. Max liked to be the judge of all the games in the park. One day, Max and Kitty had a race. Max ran fast, but Kitty stretched her legs and ran faster. Max was not happy. He wanted to be the winner. Max said, "Kitty, you can't win. I am the judge and I say I win!" Kitty was sad, but she knew she was the real winner. She went to her friends and told them what happened. All her friends said, "Kitty, you are the best at stretching and running. We know you won!" Kitty felt better and knew that Max could not always be the judge.
Write a short story about Jazz.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Jazz. Jazz loved to move and play all day. He was a very intelligent dog and had many friends. One day, Jazz saw a little bird who was sad. The bird could not fly because its wing was hurt. Jazz wanted to help the bird, so he thought of a plan. He found a soft leaf and gently put the bird on it. Then, he moved the leaf to a safe place where the bird could rest. Jazz's friends saw how he helped the bird and they were very happy. They learned that it is good to help others when they need it. Jazz was not only intelligent but also kind. And from that day on, all the animals in the forest knew the importance of helping each other.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a boy named Tim found a big piece of paper. He wanted to draw a picture for his mom. He was anxious because he wanted it to be perfect. Tim asked his sister, Sally, to help him. "Can you help me draw a picture for mom?" Tim asked. Sally said, "Yes, I will help you!" They started to draw and color the picture. They drew a big sun, a house, and a happy family. But then, something unexpected happened. Sally accidentally broke the crayon in half. Tim was upset. "Now we can't finish the picture!" he cried. Sally thought for a moment and then had an idea. "We can still use the broken crayon to finish the picture," she said. They used the broken crayon to draw more things, like flowers and trees. In the end, the picture looked even better than before. Tim and Sally gave the picture to their mom, who loved it. She hung it on the fridge for everyone to see. Tim learned that even when things break, they can still be useful and make something beautiful.
Write a short story about Lily.

One day, a girl named Lily went to the park to collect pretty rocks. She saw a boy named Tim with a big bag. Tim had a useful bag to hold his rocks. Lily wanted to be friends with Tim. "Hi, I am Lily. Can I help you find rocks?" she asked. Tim said, "Yes! Let's find rocks with spots on them." They looked and looked, but they could not find any spot rocks. Lily and Tim started to feel sad. Then, they saw a dog with a spot on its fur. The dog was digging in the ground. They went to the dog and found many spot rocks. Lily and Tim were happy and became good friends.
Write a short story about Every.

Write a short story about Molly.

Molly loved to stir. Whenever she had a bowl, she'd take a spoon and stir it around and around. One day, she found a magazine. It was full of pictures and words she could not read. But Molly saw that the magazine stir things too. She saw pictures of people stirring sugar into tea and stirring eggs for an omelette. Molly was excited! She looked around and found available pots and pans and spoons. Then she grabbed the magazine and started stirring. She stirred and stirred, from the kitchen to the living room. She had so much fun, and even sang a stir song. When Molly had stirred everything she could find, she put down her magazine. All the things had been stirred, but Molly was still excited. She knew she could find more things to stir and she started looking around the house. Soon she was stirring again, and having loads of fun!

Write a short story about Sue.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved to learn about animals. Her favorite animal was a fish. She liked to watch them swim in the water. One day, Sue went to a big pond with her mom and dad. At the pond, Sue saw a huge fish. The fish was bigger than any other fish she had ever seen. Sue was very excited. She wanted to learn more about the huge fish. She looked at the fish and saw that it was sad. Suddenly, the huge fish started to change. It was not a fish anymore. It turned into a big frog! Sue was very surprised. She did not expect the fish to become a frog. The frog was happy now. It jumped into the water and swam away. Sue learned that sometimes, things are not what they seem.
Write a short story about Hello.

Once upon a time, there was a big giant. He lived in a huge, expensive house. The giant was very lonely. He wanted to make friends. One day, a little boy came to the giant's house. The boy was not scared. He said, "Hello, I am Sam. I want to be your friend." The giant was happy. He introduced himself, "Hi Sam, I am Big Giant. Let's be friends." Big Giant and Sam played together every day. They had a lot of fun. The giant was not lonely anymore. And they lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Tom.

One day, a thin cat named Tom went to the park. He saw a broken swing and wanted to play on it. So, Tom decided to repair the swing with his small paws. Tom worked hard to fix the swing. When he was done, he jumped on the swing and began to swing high in the sky. He was very happy and proud. But then, something unexpected happened. The swing broke again, and Tom fell to the ground. He looked up and saw a big elephant named Ellie. Ellie said, "Oops, I broke the swing by accident when I tried to play on it too." Tom and Ellie became friends and fixed the swing together. They played on the swings all day, laughing and having fun.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim had a big box full of toys. One day, he saw a long tube in the box. Tim was very happy and wanted to play with the tube. Tim took the tube outside and saw his friend, Sue. Sue asked, "What do you have, Tim?" Tim said, "I have a tube. Let's play with it!" They decided to scatter small stones through the tube. They had lots of fun. But then, something unexpected happened. A small bird flew down and picked up the tube. Tim and Sue were very tired from playing, but they chased the bird to get the tube back. The bird dropped the tube, and it fell into a big puddle. Tim and Sue were sad, but they laughed and went home to find another toy to play with.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom and Lily liked to play with their toys. They had a big box of toys in their room. They had dolls, cars, blocks, books, and more. One day, they found a new toy in the box. It was a fake sheep. It was white and soft, and it had wool on its body. It also had a button on its ear. "What is this?" Tom asked, picking up the sheep. "Let me see!" Lily said, grabbing the sheep from Tom. She pressed the button on its ear. The sheep made a sound. "Baa, baa, baa!" Tom and Lily laughed. They liked the sheep. They decided to name it Fluffy. "Can we keep Fluffy?" Lily asked. "Sure, why not?" Tom said. "He is our new friend." They played with Fluffy for a while. They made him jump, run, and eat grass. They also gave him a hat and a scarf. They had fun with Fluffy. Then, Tom had an idea. He saw a record player on the shelf. It was a toy that could play music and sounds. Tom liked to record his voice and listen to it. "Hey, Lily, let's record Fluffy!" Tom said. "Record Fluffy? How?" Lily asked. "Watch me!" Tom said. He took Fluffy and the record player. He pressed the button on Fluffy's ear and the record button on the player at the same time. Fluffy said, "Baa, baa, baa!" and the player recorded it. "Wow, that's cool!" Lily said. Tom pressed the play button on the player. Fluffy's voice came out. "Baa, baa, baa!" Tom and Lily laughed again. They liked to hear Fluffy's voice. They recorded Fluffy many times. They made him say different things. They made him say, "Hello, I am Fluffy!" and "I love Tom and Lily!" and "I am a happy sheep!" They had a lot of fun with Fluffy and the record player. They played with them until it was time for dinner. They put Fluffy and the record player back in the box. They hugged Fluffy and said goodbye. "See you tomorrow, Fluffy!" Tom said. "We love you, Fluffy!" Lily said. Fluffy smiled and said, "Baa, baa, baa!"
Write a short story about John.

One day, John went for a walk in a very hot place. It was so hot that he was sweating and scratching an itch on his arm. Then he heard someone talk. John stopped to listen. It was an old man's voice and he was saying, "Beware, it's a dangerous path ahead!" But John was curious and he wanted to know what was so dangerous, so he decided to keep walking. As he kept walking, the heat got even hotter and the itch on his arm got worse. Finally, he reached the end of the path and he saw the old man. But he was too late to learn why the old man had warned him - a giant fire was about to engulf John! John screamed for help but the fire was too big and too hot. He was never seen again. The old man had been right - he should have listened!
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily and Ben were playing in the pool. They liked to splash each other with water. They had a lot of fun. But then, Ben saw a big bowl of celery on the table. He loved celery. He wanted to eat some. He got out of the pool and ran to the table. "Hey, where are you going?" Lily asked. She followed him. Ben grabbed a piece of celery and bit it. But it did not taste good. It tasted like plastic. He spat it out. "Yuck! This is not celery. This is fake celery!" he said. Lily laughed. She knew it was fake celery. She had put it there as a joke. She wanted to trick Ben. "Ha ha, I got you! That is not real celery. That is fake celery. I made it with paper and paint. You are so silly!" she said. Ben was not happy. He felt angry and fooled. He threw the fake celery at Lily. It hit her in the face. "Ow! That hurt!" she said. She threw some water at Ben. He threw some water back at her. They started to splash each other again. But this time, they were not having fun. They were having a fight.
Write a short story about Mia.

One day, a little girl named Mia was playing in her room. She had many toys, but her room was not organized. Her mom told her, "Mia, you must clean up your toys." Mia did not want to clean up her toys. She wanted to go outside and play with her friends. So, she left her room and went outside. She played all day and had fun. When Mia came home, she saw her toys all over her room. She felt regret for not cleaning up her toys. She said to her mom, "I am sorry, Mom. I will clean up my room now." Mia and her mom cleaned up the room together. They made the room organized and nice. Mia was happy to have a clean room and promised to keep it that way.
Write a short story about Soon.

One day, a planet was feeling very unhappy. It felt too big and alone in the sky. So one day, it decided to shrink! As the planet got smaller and smaller, the sky around it seemed harsher and colder. Soon it felt snug and safe. Finally, the planet was the perfect size. It even made a few friends, like the stars and the moon. Life was much better now that the planet had shrunk.
Write a short story about Buddy.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful dog named Buddy. Buddy loved to play with his friends and help them in any way he could. One day, Buddy saw a little bird who was sad because her nest was on a very high post. Buddy knew he had to do something to help the little bird. So, he tried to climb the post to get the nest. But the post was too tall and slippery for Buddy. He tried and tried, but he could not reach the nest. Buddy felt very sad because he could not help his friend. Then, Buddy had an idea. He went to his friend, the wise old owl. The owl told Buddy that if he behaved well and listened to his friends, they would help him too. Buddy went back to the post and asked his friends to help him. Together, they were able to get the nest down for the little bird. The bird was very happy, and Buddy learned that if you behave and are helpful, your friends will help you too.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim was restless. He could not sit still. He wanted to play all day. One day, Tim's mom said, "We will have a party!" Tim was very happy. He could not wait for the party to happen. He told his friend, Sam. Sam said, "I will come too!" On the day of the party, Tim and Sam played games, ate cake, and had lots of fun. The party happened, and Tim was not restless anymore. They were both happy and tired after the party.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a big castle. In the castle, there lived a king and queen. They were very nice and always tried to keep everyone warm and happy. One day, a big dragon came to the castle. The dragon was not nice. The king and queen were scared. They tried to keep the dragon away, but the dragon was too strong. In the end, the dragon took over the castle. The king and queen had to leave their home. They were very sad. The castle was not warm and happy anymore.
Write a short story about Sue.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved her aunt. Her aunt was very nice. They liked to play together. One day, Sue's aunt came to visit her. Sue's aunt wanted to teach Sue something new. She had a big bag with her. She opened the bag and took out a lemon. The lemon was yellow and round. Sue looked at the lemon and was curious. Sue's aunt cut the lemon and gave a piece to Sue. She told Sue it was sour. Sue tasted the lemon. It was very sour, but Sue liked it. She smiled and said "thank you" to her aunt. They laughed and enjoyed the sour lemon together.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a small, ugly house with a big roof. In this house, there lived a happy cat named Tom and a little girl named Sue. They loved to play together and have fun. One sunny day, Tom and Sue were playing in the yard. Tom was very excited to see a big, red ball. He jumped high in the air and hit the ball with his paw. The ball flew up, up, up and got stuck on the roof of the ugly house. "Oh no!" said Sue, "How will we get the ball down?" Tom looked up at the roof and had an idea. He climbed up the tree near the house and jumped onto the roof. He pushed the big, red ball with his paw and it rolled down. "Thank you, Tom!" Sue said with a big smile. They were both excited to play with the ball again. From that day on, Tom and Sue had many more fun adventures in their ugly house with the big roof.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a boy named Tim went to the park. He saw a big tree with a lot of fruit on it. Tim thought it would be interesting to pick the fruit. He tried to reach the fruit, but he was too small. Tim was sad because he could not pick the fruit. Then, Tim had an idea. He went home and got his toy gun that could shoot soft balls. Tim thought he could use the toy gun to shoot the fruit down. He went back to the park and tried to shoot the fruit. As he shot the fruit, something unexpected happened. A big, friendly dog jumped up and caught the fruit in its mouth. The dog was happy to help Tim pick the fruit. Tim and the dog became good friends, and they played at the park every day.
Write a short story about Ben.

Once upon a time, there was a big bull named Ben. Ben lived on a farm with his friends. He was a lucky bull because he had lots of food and a nice place to sleep. One day, Ben saw a little girl named Sue. Sue had a bag with a zip. Ben was curious and asked, "What's in the bag, Sue?" Sue smiled and said, "I have a surprise for you, Ben!" Sue opened the bag with a zip sound. Inside the bag, there was a big, red ball. Ben was so happy to see the ball. He played with the ball all day with Sue. They had a lot of fun together. And that's how Ben and Sue became best friends.
Write a short story about Let.

One day, a cat, a dog, and a bird were playing in the park. They saw a different kind of rope on the ground. The cat said, "Let's play with the rope!" The dog said, "How can we prevent the rope from getting lost?" The bird thought for a moment and said, "We can tie it to a tree, so it won't go away." So, the cat, the dog, and the bird tied the rope to a tree and started playing. They played all day, and they were very happy. At the end of the day, they untied the rope and went back to their homes.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom and Sam are friends. They like to play in the park. One day, they find a big beetle. It is shiny and green. They think it is pretty. "Let's use the beetle for a game," Tom says. "We can pretend it is a jewel. We are rich and we have a treasure." "OK," Sam says. "But we have to be careful. We don't want to hurt the beetle. It is alive and it can fly." They take the beetle to a bench. They put it in a box. They make up a story. They are kings and they have a castle. The beetle is their magic jewel. It can make their wishes come true. They have fun with the beetle. They wish for candy, toys, and books. They share their wishes with each other. They are happy and kind. But then, the beetle starts to move. It wants to get out of the box. It makes a noise. It flaps its wings. "Look, the beetle is awake," Tom says. "It wants to go home. We should let it go." "Yes, you are right," Sam says. "We had fun with the beetle, but it is not ours. It belongs to the park. We should say thank you and goodbye." They open the box. They hold the beetle gently. They say thank you and goodbye. They watch the beetle fly away. They smile and wave. They are still friends. They are still rich. They have each other. And they have a memory.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big mouth and liked to poke things. One day, she saw a green frog by the pond. The frog was envious of Lily's big mouth. It wanted to have a big mouth too, so it could eat more bugs. Lily saw the frog and poked it with a stick. The frog jumped high into the air and landed on Lily's head. Lily laughed and the frog laughed too. They became good friends and played by the pond every day. One day, when they were playing, the frog jumped into Lily's big mouth. Lily was surprised and didn't know what to do. But then, the frog jumped back out and started to laugh. The frog didn't want a big mouth anymore. It was happy just the way it was. And Lily and the frog stayed friends, playing and laughing together by the pond.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He had a toy frog that he loved very much. One day, Tim and his frog went to play outside. They had lots of fun in the sun. While playing, Tim saw a big bug. The bug was green and disgusting. Tim was scared and hid under a big tree. The bug came close to Tim, and he did not like it. He wanted the bug to go away. Tim remembered that his toy frog was brave. He held his frog tight and gave it a kiss. Suddenly, the frog came to life and jumped on the bug. The bug was scared and ran away. Tim was happy and thanked his frog with another kiss. They played together all day long.
Write a short story about Lulu.

Once upon a time there were two best friends, Lulu and Daisy. Every Sunday they would go to the park and have so much fun. They would swing on the swings, race with each other and rock on the rocking chairs. One day, Lulu wanted to rock alone. She thought it was more fun if no one was with her. Daisy was jealous but she said, "Okay, I'll wait for you!" When Lulu was done rocking, Daisy had a brilliant idea. "Let's rock together!" she said. Lulu was excited to try it. With their arms linked, they started to rock in sync. It was so much better than rocking alone. The moral of the story is that two is always better than one. You can do more fun things and have a better time when you have a friend to join you.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a little boy named Tim wanted to do something fun. He saw a big box in his room. Inside the box, he found many gears. Tim was very happy. He wanted to play with the gears and make a fun toy. Tim tried to put the gears together, but it was hard. He felt frustrated. He asked his mom for help. She told him to keep trying and not give up. She said that soon he would be able to do it. Tim kept trying and trying. He did not give up. Soon, he was able to put the gears together. He made a fun toy that could move. Tim was very happy. He learned that if he did not give up, he could do anything.
Write a short story about Emma.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Emma. Emma liked to raise her hand in class. One day, her teacher told the class they would have a march outside. Emma was very excited. Emma and her friend, Lily, talked about the march. "I want to wear my pretty dress," said Emma. "That's a good idea," said Lily. "It's very attractive." So, Emma wore her pretty dress to the march. During the march, Emma and Lily walked and smiled. They had so much fun. The other kids saw Emma's attractive dress and wanted to be her friend. They all learned that being nice and sharing good ideas can make more friends. And they all lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Tom.

One day, Tom went to the farm with his mom. The farm was big and had many animals. Tom was very excite to see all the animals. The sky was gray and the ground was wet. Tom wore his boots so his feet would not get wet. Tom saw a pig in the mud. The pig said, "Hi, Tom! I like the wet mud. It is fun to play in." Tom said, "Hi, pig! I like to play too!" They both laughed and played in the wet mud. After playing, Tom and his mom went home. Tom was happy he made a new friend at the farm. He could not wait to go back to the farm and play with the pig again.
Write a short story about Can.

Once upon a time, there was a little plant. The plant was green and lived in a big garden. It loved to relax in the sun. One day, a girl saw the plant. She thought it looked tasty. She wanted to eat the plant, but she knew she should ask her mom first. The girl went to her mom and asked, "Can I eat this tasty plant?" Her mom said, "Yes, you can." The girl ate the plant and it was very tasty. They both smiled and went to relax in the sun.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a big bear who loved to bake. He had a little bear friend who wanted to learn how to bake too. So, the big bear said, "I will teach you how to bake, little bear!" One day, they went to the woods to find a special tree. The big bear told the little bear that this tree had a secret. The tree looked dead, but it was not. The big bear said, "This tree will help us bake something very yummy." The big bear and the little bear took a piece of the tree and went back home. They used the piece of the tree in their baking. The little bear was so happy to learn from the big bear. They baked and baked, and then they had a big party with all their friends. The secret of the dead-looking tree made their baking the best ever, and everyone loved it.
Write a short story about Hello.

Once upon a time, there was a little cricket. The cricket had smooth wings. He liked to play all day. One day, he met a big ant. "Hello, ant," said the cricket. "Do you want to play with me?" "No," said the ant. "I must work. If I do not work, I will be punished." The cricket did not want the ant to be punished. So, he helped the ant work. They moved food and cleaned their home. They worked hard all day. At the end of the day, the ant thanked the cricket. "Thank you for helping me," said the ant. "Now I will not be punished." The cricket was happy to help. They became good friends. And they played together when their work was done.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a child named Tim. Tim was incredible at making friends. One day, Tim went to the park to play. At the park, Tim saw a girl who was weeping. She looked sad. Tim walked up to her and asked, "Why are you weeping?" The girl said, "I lost my toy." Tim wanted to help. He looked all around the park. He found the toy under a big tree. The girl was so happy. She said, "Thank you, Tim!" From that day on, Tim and the girl were best friends. They played together every day, and they never felt sad again.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once there was a clever animal. His name was Tim. He lived on a farm and was best friends with an old farmer, Jim. Every morning Jim would come to the farm and have a chat with Tim. One day Jim said to Tim "It's a beautiful day, I think we should go and explore!" Tim was very excited and with a leap and a bound, he started running around the farm. Suddenly, something strange caught Tim's eye. In the farmyard was a small rabbit. It was shaking with fear. Tim crept slowly towards the rabbit and said, "Don't be afraid." The rabbit was so scared it couldn't move. So Tim gently kissed the rabbit on the head, and the rabbit was filled with courage and trust. Jim watched from the sidelines with admiration. He smiled and said, "Tim you are so clever. A little act of kindness can go a long way." This special moment taught Tim two important lessons - that kindness is powerful, and that the best way of showing kindness to another creature is through a gentle kiss.
Write a short story about Spot.

One day, a cheerful little dog named Spot found a big sack. Spot wanted to know what was inside the sack. He tried to open it, but it was too hard for him. He needed help. Spot went to his friend, a strong cat named Tom. "Tom, I found a sack. Can you help me open it?" asked Spot. Tom wanted to help his friend, so they both went to the sack. When they got to the sack, Tom tried to open it too. But it was still too hard for both of them. They needed more help. They went to their friend, a wise bird named Polly. Polly looked at the sack and said, "You need to work together to open it." Spot and Tom tried again, this time working together. They pulled and pulled, and finally, the sack opened. Inside, they found a lot of yummy treats. They were so happy and shared the treats with all their friends.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a little boy named Tim went for a walk with his mom. They walked down the street, holding hands. The sun was shining, and the birds were singing. As they walked, they saw a huge billboard. The billboard had a picture of a big, red ball on it. Tim pointed at the billboard and said, "Look, Mom! Big, red ball!" His mom smiled and said, "Yes, Tim, that's a big, red ball on the billboard." They kept walking and saw a little girl playing with a ball that looked just like the one on the billboard. Tim asked his mom, "Can I play too?" His mom said, "Of course, Tim. Go have fun!" So Tim played with the little girl and her big, red ball, and they all had a great day.
Write a short story about Ducky.

Once upon a time, there was a little duck named Ducky. Ducky lived in a pond with his family. One day, Ducky found an unusual big rock near the pond. He was very curious about the rock. Every day, Ducky would go to the rock and bathe in the water around it. He loved playing in the water and splashing it with his wings. The other ducks would laugh and say, "Silly Ducky, that's just a rock!" But Ducky knew there was something special about the rock. One hot day, the pond started to dry up. All the ducks were thirsty and scared. They didn't know what to do. Ducky thought about the unusual rock and led his family to it. When they got there, they found a secret spring of water hidden behind the rock! The ducks were so happy and thanked Ducky for finding it. From that day on, they all knew that even unusual things can be very important. The moral of the story is: don't judge something just because it looks different.
Write a short story about Let.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl. She was very helpless. She wanted to find her way to her bedroom. Then, there was a kind man who offered to help her. He said, "Let me lead you to your bedroom." The little girl happily accepted his help. She said, "Thank you very much!" The kind man held her hand and led her to her bedroom. When they reached her bedroom, the little girl was very excited. She said, "This is my bedroom!" She was so happy that she had been able to find her way with the help of the kind man. She hugged him and thanked him again. The kind man smiled and said, "You're very welcome!" He waved goodbye and the little girl went into her bedroom. She was no longer helpless!
Write a short story about Sam.

Sam and Mia were playing in the park. They saw a big bottle on the grass. It was shiny and blue. They ran to the bottle and picked it up. "Look, it has a cork!" Sam said. "Maybe there is something inside." "Let's open it and see!" Mia said. She tried to pull the cork, but it was too tight. She could not make it move. "Here, let me try!" Sam said. He took the bottle from Mia and pushed the cork with his thumb. He pushed hard and hard, until the cork popped out. "Wow, you did it!" Mia said. She looked inside the bottle. There was nothing but air. "Maybe it's magic!" Sam said. "Maybe we can make a wish!" "Okay, let's try!" Mia said. She closed her eyes and said, "I wish for a big cake!" Sam closed his eyes and said, "I wish for a new bike!" They opened their eyes and waited. But nothing happened. The bottle was still empty. "That's not fair!" Sam said. He was angry. He threw the bottle on the ground. It broke into many pieces. "Hey, don't do that!" Mia said. She was scared. She saw a man coming towards them. He was wearing a uniform and a hat. He looked angry too. "Who are you?" Sam asked. "I'm the park ranger," the man said. "And you are in big trouble. Do you know what you did? You released a rare bird that was in that bottle. It was part of a conservation project. Now it's gone and we can't find it. You also littered the park with glass. That's very dangerous and bad for the environment. You should be ashamed of yourselves." Sam and Mia felt very sorry. They did not know about the bird. They did not mean to be bad. They wanted to say sorry, but they could not find the words. They started to cry. The man shook his head. He took them by the hand and led them to his car. He said he would take them to their parents and tell them what they did. He said they would have to pay a fine and do some community service. He said they would learn their lesson. Sam and Mia wished they never found the bottle. They wished they never made a wish. They wished they could go back and do things differently. But it was too late. They had made a big mistake. And they had to face the consequences.
Write a short story about Why.

Once upon a time, there was a big, impressive shark. He liked to swim in the sea and play with his friends. One day, he saw a little fish who was sad. The shark swam to the fish and asked, "Why are you sad?" The little fish said, "I lost my family. Can you help me find them?" The shark agreed and told the fish to follow him. As they swam together, the shark told the fish to stay close so he wouldn't get lost again. Then, they met a wise old turtle. The turtle said, "I can help you find your family, but first, you must do something for me." The shark and the fish listened. The turtle told the shark to order his friends to help clean the sea. The shark was surprised but agreed to help. The shark and his friends cleaned the sea, and the turtle showed the fish where his family was. The fish was happy to be with his family again. The shark learned that helping others can make you feel good too. From that day on, the shark and his friends kept the sea clean and played happily together.
Write a short story about Every.

Once there was a youth who loved to sleep. Every night he would close his eyes, and drift away peacefully. But then one day, something terrible happened. His bedroom became incredibly boring! Nothing exciting ever happened. The youth talked to his parents about it. "It's so dull in here," he said. "I'm so bored!" His parents thought for a moment. Then his father had an idea. "Let's make it fun!" he suggested. So, they worked together to decorate the room. They painted the walls bright colors and hung fun decorations from the ceiling. The youth was amazed! The room was now fun and exciting, and the youth loved it. He said goodnight to his parents and drifted off to sleep. He had the most wonderful dreams and was so happy he'd made his room more exciting.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved candy very much. One day, he went to the store with his mom to buy some candy. Tim was very careful when he picked out his candy. He said, "Thank you, Mom!" when she bought it for him. On their way home, Tim saw a little dog. The dog looked sad and hungry. Tim thought about his candy and wanted to share it with the dog. He gave the dog a piece of candy, and the dog wagged its tail. Tim felt happy and said, "You're welcome, dog!" But then, something unexpected happened! The dog started to change. It grew bigger and bigger until it was a giant dog! Tim was surprised, but he wasn't scared. The giant dog was very friendly. It licked Tim's face and wagged its tail. Tim knew that he had made a new friend. From that day on, Tim and the giant dog went on many fun adventures together.
Write a short story about Amy.

One day, a sweet little girl named Amy went to the park with her mom. Amy loved to play with her toy model car. It was her favorite toy. She would push it around and make it go fast. Today, she brought her car to the park to play. While Amy was playing, she saw a big ice cream truck. The ice cream truck had many cold and sweet treats. Amy asked her mom if she could have an ice cream. Her mom said yes, and Amy got a big, sweet ice cream cone. As Amy was eating her ice cream, something unexpected happened. The ice cream started to freeze her toy model car! Amy didn't know what to do. She tried to pull the car out, but it was stuck in the ice cream. Then, the ice cream truck man came over and helped her. He had a special tool to get the car out of the ice cream. Amy was so happy, and she learned that ice cream can freeze things. She played with her car and ate her sweet ice cream, and had a great day at the park.
Write a short story about Suddenly.

Once upon a time, a chubby bird was flying in the sky. Suddenly, it spotted a big, shiny automobile on the ground. The bird trembled, because it was so scared. It flew away quickly and found a safe spot in a tree. The bird stayed there for a while, watching the automobile. After a few minutes, the automobile started the engine and drove away. The bird trembled with relief and flew off on its journey. The bird kept flying, until it reached its nest. It was filled with warm, cozy feathers. The bird snuggled in and fell asleep. The next morning, the bird was excited to go explore the world again. Its chubby body felt strong and brave, so it hopped out of the nest and ventured off into the blue sky.
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a big, cold freezer in her house. Lucy loved to speak with her mom about the perfect ice cream they kept in the freezer. One sunny day, Lucy's mom went to the store. Lucy wanted to eat the perfect ice cream. She opened the freezer, but it was very high. She tried to reach the ice cream, but she was too small. Lucy got a chair to stand on, but it was not safe. Lucy stood on the chair and opened the freezer. She got the perfect ice cream and was so happy. But when she tried to get down, she fell off the chair. Lucy was very sad and hurt. She learned not to climb on chairs when her mom was not there to help her.
Write a short story about Rudy.

Once upon a time, there was a reindeer named Rudy. Rudy was a careless reindeer. He loved to run and play with his friends. One day, Rudy and his friends were playing in the forest. They were all having fun and enjoying the sunny day. Rudy's friend, a squirrel named Sammy, said, "Rudy, be careful! You might trip and fall!" But Rudy didn't listen. He was too busy enjoying the game they were playing. Suddenly, Rudy tripped on a rock and fell down. His friends gathered around him, worried. Rudy said, "I'm okay, but I should have listened to you, Sammy. I was careless." From that day on, Rudy learned to be more careful while playing, but he still enjoyed having fun with his friends.
Write a short story about Remember.

Once upon a time, there was a pretty bee. The bee lived in a big hive with her bee friends. They all worked together to make sweet honey. One day, the pretty bee went out to find flowers. She flew and flew, looking for the best flowers. She found a lot of flowers, but she forgot where her hive was. She felt sad and didn't know what to do. Then, the pretty bee saw her friend, the butterfly. The butterfly said, "Remember, your hive is near the big tree." The pretty bee was so happy! She flew back to her hive and told her friends about her adventure. They all laughed and shared the sweet honey they made.
Write a short story about Tina.

Once there was a small animal named Tina who wanted to go on an adventure. She was very excited and couldn’t wait to explore the world. As Tina walked along the path, she noticed a big button. She wanted to push it, but had a feeling it might be a bad idea. However, her curiosity got the best of her and Tina went ahead and pressed the button. Suddenly a loud noise filled the air and Tina started to panic. After Tina had calmed down, she noticed that the button was broken. Tina was sad and angry at the same time. She realized that if she hadn’t been so curious she wouldn’t have broken the button. At that moment, Tina understood the moral of the story: sometimes it’s better to be safe than sorry. She decided that the next time she had a feeling of caution, she would think twice before acting upon it.
Write a short story about Tim.
