Lily wanted to eat them. Lily walked to the tree and tried to grab a peach. But the peaches were too high for her. She jumped and jumped, but she could not reach them. She was sad and still hungry. Then, a nice tall boy named Tom came by. He saw Lily and asked, "Why are you sad?" Lily told him about the peaches. Tom helped her by grabbing a peach from the tree. Lily was so happy and said, "Thank you, Tom!" They both sat under the tree and ate the peaches. They were not hungry anymore.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a big forest. In the forest, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim liked to follow his dad when he went to cut wood. One day, Tim and his dad went into the forest. Tim's dad had a heavy axe to cut the wood. Tim's dad said, "Tim, you stay here. I will go cut wood." Tim sat on a rock and watched his dad go. But Tim wanted to help, so he picked up a small stick and started to follow his dad. Tim saw his dad cut a big tree with the heavy axe. Tim tried to help by hitting the tree with his small stick. His dad smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim. You are a good helper!" Tim and his dad went home with the wood, happy to have spent the day together in the forest.
Write a short story about Max.

Once upon a time, there was a fast dog named Max. Max loved to run and play all day. He was so fast that no one could catch him. Max was very brave and would dare to do anything. One day, Max was playing in the park when he found a big bone. He dared to dig it up and take it home. Max was so excited about his new bone. But, Max was running too fast and he tripped on a rock. Max was hurt and needed to see a doctor. The doctor took Max to a special room with a big machine. The machine was called an x-ray. The x-ray showed Max's leg to see if it was broken. The doctor looked at the x-ray and said, "Max, you are a very lucky dog. Your leg is not broken, but you need to be more careful when you run fast." Max was happy and promised to be more careful when he played.
Write a short story about Toby.

Toby was a three year old who enjoyed going to the movies. Every Saturday, Toby went to the theater with his dad and they chose a movie to watch. This week they choose an original movie that they’ve never seen before. Toby was so excited — he couldn’t wait to see the movie! As soon as the movie began, Toby was entranced. He watched with joy as the characters danced across the screen and sang beautiful songs. The movie continued and Toby became more and more excited — as it went on, he noticed that the plot seemed very familiar. By the time the movie reached it's climax, Toby knew what was about to happen — and sure enough, the ending was just like Toby had predicted! Toby couldn’t believe it — the original movie he had chosen was the same movie he watched every week at the theater! Toby was really proud that he had chosen the original movie. He smiled and looked over at his dad with a look of accomplishment. His dad smiled too, proud that Toby had chosen so wisely.
Write a short story about Finally.

Once upon a time there was a girl who loved to go for walks. On this day, she decided to go for a walk in the park. When she arrived she was amazed by the impressive trees and beautiful flowers. She looked around and saw a big lake. She got closer the the lake and saw a cute duck swimming in the lake. She was so happy and excited that she started looking at the duck closely. She admired the duck's beautiful feathers which were so soft and had a nice texture. She said to the duck, "You are so beautiful. I admire you so much!" The duck was so happy that it started swimming faster and making fun sounds. The girl laughed and was happy to see how impressed the duck was. Finally, she hugged the duck and said goodbye. After that, she started to walk back home with a smile knowing that the duck was happy.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to ride his bike. Every day, he would ride up and down the street, feeling very proud of himself. Tim's mom would watch him ride and clap her hands, happy to see her son having fun. One day, Tim saw his friend Sally sitting on the sidewalk, crying. Sally had dropped her pastry on the ground, and she was very sad. Tim wanted to help Sally feel better, so he had an idea. He went to his mom and asked for a pastry. Tim's mom gave him a pastry, and he rode his bike to Sally. He gave the pastry to Sally, and she stopped crying. Sally was so happy that she hugged Tim. They both felt good inside, knowing that sharing and helping friends is important. From that day on, Tim and Sally always helped each other and shared their things. They learned that being kind and sharing with friends makes everyone happy. And every time Tim rode his bike, he felt proud to be a good friend.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little girl named Lily and her mom. Lily had a wiggly tooth. She was very excited about it. She wanted her tooth to come out so the Tooth Fairy would come and visit her. One day, Lily's mom was trying to put on her shoe, but it was too tight. It did not fit. "Oh no, my shoe won't fit!" she said. Lily saw her mom needed help. She went to her mom and said, "Let me help you, Mom!" Lily pushed the shoe, and it fit her mom's foot. Her mom was happy and thanked Lily. Later that day, Lily's wiggly tooth fell out. She showed her mom, and they both laughed. Lily put the tooth under her pillow and went to sleep. That night, the Tooth Fairy came and took the tooth. She left a shiny coin for Lily. In the morning, Lily found the coin and was so happy. She showed her mom, and they both knew the Tooth Fairy had visited.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom and Mia liked to pretend. They pretended to be pirates, or astronauts, or superheroes. They used their toys and their imagination to make up stories and adventures. One day, they found a large glass in the kitchen. It was bigger than their heads, and shiny and clear. They wondered what they could pretend with it. Tom had an idea. He said, "Let's pretend this is a magic glass. It can show us anything we want to see." Mia liked the idea. She said, "OK, what do you want to see?" Tom thought for a moment. He said, "I want to see a dinosaur. A big, scary dinosaur." He held the glass in front of his face and looked inside. He made a roar and said, "Look, there's a dinosaur. It's coming to get us. Run!" Mia laughed and ran with him. She said, "My turn, my turn. I want to see a fairy. A pretty, sparkly fairy." She took the glass from Tom and looked inside. She made a tinkling sound and said, "Look, there's a fairy. She's flying to us. She wants to be our friend. Come!" Tom smiled and followed her. They played with the glass for a long time, pretending to see different things. They had fun and forgot about everything else. But they did not notice that their mom was watching them. She had a surprise for them. She had bought them a new book, with pictures of dinosaurs and fairies and many other things. She waited for them to finish playing, and then she said, "Tom, Mia, I have something for you. Come and see." They ran to her and saw the book. They were very happy and excited. They said, "Wow, thank you, mom. This is the best book ever. Can you read it to us?" Mom said, "Of course, I can. But first, let's put the glass back in the kitchen. It's not a toy, you know. It's for drinking juice or water." Tom and Mia nodded. They said, "OK, mom. We're sorry. We were just pretending." Mom said, "I know, and that's fine. Pretending is good. It helps you learn and have fun. But you also have to be careful and respectful with things that are not yours. Do you understand?" Tom and Mia said, "Yes, mom. We understand. We love you." Mom said, "I love you too. Now, let's go and read the book. I'm sure you'll find many more things to pretend with."
Write a short story about Spot.

Once upon a time, there was a brave dog named Spot. Spot had a job. His job was to help people. He liked his job a lot. One day, Spot met a little girl named Lily. Lily was lost and scared. She said, "Can you help me, Spot? I can't find my mom." Spot wagged his tail and said, "Yes, I can help you, Lily." Spot and Lily walked and walked. They looked for Lily's mom. Soon, they found her. Lily hugged her mom and said, "Thank you, Spot, for being so brave and helping me." Spot was happy he did his job well.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a wise cat named Tom. Tom lived in a big house with his best friend, a little girl named Lily. They liked to play together every day at noon. One day, they were playing in the garden when a new cat named Max came to visit. Tom said to Max, "Do you want to play with us?" Max nodded and said, "Yes, I would like that." So, Tom, Lily, and Max played together until they were all very tired. They had so much fun! Then, something unexpected happened. Max offered Tom and Lily a big, shiny toy. But when they tried to play with it, the toy broke into many pieces! Max felt very sad and said, "I'm sorry, I didn't know it would break." Tom and Lily smiled and said, "That's okay, Max. We can still play together without the toy." And they all played happily ever after.
Write a short story about Bob.

One day, Bob and Liz were playing outside. Liz was pushing Bob on the swing. It was really hot outside, and they were both sweating. Suddenly, Bob heard a loud sound coming from the tree in their yard. When they looked closer, they saw a dead bird lying on the ground. Bob and Liz were both scared. They didn't know what to do. But then, Bob had an idea. He quickly grabbed a stick and started poking the bird with it. He wanted to see if it would move. That's when the bird suddenly opened its eyes and started repeating the same thing over and over again - "hot, hot, hot!" Bob and Liz were shocked. They had just awoken the dead bird with their stick! Bob quickly grabbed Liz and they ran inside the house. From then on, Bob and Liz knew that it was important not to repeat the same thing over and over again.
Write a short story about Tim.

Tim and Sue wanted to play in the old store. They liked to look at the old things and pretend they were new. But their mom said no. She said the store was dirty and dangerous. She said there were many cobwebs and rats and broken glass. She said they could get hurt or sick. But Tim and Sue did not listen. They waited until mom was busy and ran to the store. They opened the door and went inside. It was dark and dusty. They saw many cobwebs on the walls and ceiling. They saw some rats run away. They saw some broken glass on the floor. They did not care. They thought it was fun. They found some old clothes and hats and shoes. They tried them on and laughed. They found some old toys and books and games. They played with them and had fun. They did not see the sign that said "Danger! Do not enter!" They did not hear the creaking sound. They did not feel the floor shaking. They did not notice the crack in the wall. They did not know the store was going to fall. The store fell down with a loud noise. Tim and Sue screamed. They were trapped under the rubble. They could not move. They could not see. They could not breathe. They were scared and hurt and sad. Their mom heard the noise. She ran to the store. She saw the dust and smoke. She saw the rubble and the sign. She cried and cried. She called for help. But it was too late. Tim and Sue were gone.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She loved to play with her toys. One day, Lily's mom told her they were going on a trip. Lily was very happy. She had to pack her bag with her toys and clothes. Lily saw a fake cactus in her room. She thought it was funny and put it in her bag too. When they got to their trip, Lily and her family went to the park. Lily took out her toys and the fake cactus to play. A boy named Tim saw the fake cactus and thought it was real. He was scared to touch it because real cacti are prickly. Lily showed Tim that it was fake and they both laughed. They played together and became friends. The moral of the story is to not be scared of things that look different, because they might be fun and make new friends.
Write a short story about Sam.

Once upon a time, there was a kind cat named Sam. Sam loved to drive his little red car. He would drive it up and down the street, waving to all his friends. One day, Sam saw a crack on the road. He did not want his friends to get hurt by the crack. So, he decided to help them. Sam drove to the store and got some things to fix the crack. Sam worked hard to fix the crack. Soon, the road was smooth again. His friends were very happy and thanked Sam for being so kind. They all played together and had a fun day. And Sam was proud that he could help his friends.
Write a short story about All.

One day, a brilliant rainbow came to the sky. It was big and bright. All the kids loved to look at it. They would point and say, "Wow, look at the pretty colors!" The rainbow was very happy. But one day, the rainbow was gone. The kids were sad. They did not know where the rainbow went. They looked and looked, but it was not in the sky. The kids missed the rainbow very much. Then, one day, the rainbow returned. But it was not the same. It was a twisty rainbow! The kids were so surprised. They had never seen a twisty rainbow before. They laughed and clapped. The twisty rainbow was happy, and so were the kids.
Write a short story about Max.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a brown dog named Max. Max loved to play and have fun. One day, Max saw a big party happening in the park. He wanted to go and join the fun. Max went to the party and saw many friends. He saw a cat named Lucy. Lucy said, "Hi Max, do you want to play with us?" Max was happy and said, "Yes, I want to play!" They played games, ate yummy food, and danced. Then, a new dog appeared at the party. The new dog was shy and scared. Max saw the new dog and said, "Hi, I am Max. What is your name?" The new dog said, "I am Sam." Max asked Sam to play with them. Sam was happy and played with Max and Lucy. At the end of the day, Max, Lucy, and Sam were tired but happy. They learned that being kind and making new friends is a good thing. They promised to always be nice to new friends and have fun together.
Write a short story about Mia.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Mia was a nervous girl who did not like loud noises or big crowds. She loved to play with her toys and stretch her arms up high like a big tree. One sunny day, Mia's mom asked her to help set the table for lunch. "Mia, please put this glass on the table," said her mom. Mia was scared to hold the glass because it was shiny and looked like it could break. She held it very carefully and walked slowly to the table. As Mia put the glass down, a funny thing happened. A tiny bird flew through the open window and landed on the glass. Mia was so surprised that she laughed out loud. The bird didn't seem nervous at all. It stretched its wings and flew around the room. Mia's mom and dad came in, and they all laughed together. The little bird had made Mia forget her fears, and now she was not nervous to hold a glass anymore.
Write a short story about Bobo.

Once upon a time, there was a grumpy bear named Bobo. Bobo lived in a small house in the woods. One day, Bobo found a flag outside his house. The flag was dirty, so Bobo decided to wipe it clean. Bobo's friend, a little bird named Chirpy, saw him cleaning the flag. Chirpy asked, "What are you doing, Bobo?" Bobo replied, "I found a flag and I'm wiping it clean." Chirpy wanted to help, so they both wiped the flag together. After they finished, they put the flag up on a tall tree. Bobo and Chirpy were so happy to see the clean flag waving in the wind. They danced and played under the flag all day. The grumpy bear wasn't grumpy anymore, thanks to his friend Chirpy and their new, clean flag.
Write a short story about Jack.

Once upon a time there were two siblings, Jack and Jill. They had gone to the park to play. Jack said to Jill, "Let's go play jazz!" Jill replied, "That sounds like a lot of fun! I have a plan, let's have a jazz party!" The siblings smiled at each other and ran off to find some friends to join the party. Jack was feeling extra bossy, so he shouted at his friends, "Come on, hurry up!" Jack and Jill had assembled quite a few friends, and everyone was ready to get the party started. Jack had his saxophone, Jill had her clarinet, and the rest of the kids had their tambourines and drums, and it sounded like all the angels in heaven were singing. Everyone was having a great time dancing and playing all the jazz music. The party went on for a few hours, and when it was all over, the friends agreed it was the best jazz party ever. Jack was very proud of himself for being so bossy and getting everyone together.
Write a short story about After.

Once upon a time, there was a little pen. The pen was blue and it liked to draw. The pen had many friends. They would play and have fun all day. One day, the pen was very hungry. It needed to draw more to be happy. So, the pen went to find some paper. The pen found a big, white paper and started to draw. It drew a sun, a tree, and a house. The pen was very happy. After a while, the pen was not hungry anymore. It had drawn a beautiful picture. The pen showed the picture to its friends. They all loved it. The pen was happy and proud. And they all played together again.
Write a short story about Sara.

Sara likes to play with her dolls. She has many dolls with different clothes and hair. She likes to brush their hair and make them look pretty. But today, she wants to try something new. She wants to make their hair wild. She takes her scissors and goes to her room. She kneels on the floor and picks up a doll. She cuts the doll's hair very short and messy. She thinks it looks funny. She laughs and picks up another doll. She cuts the doll's hair very long and curly. She thinks it looks silly. She laughs and picks up another doll. She cuts the doll's hair in different colors and shapes. She thinks it looks crazy. She laughs and picks up another doll. But then, she hears a voice. It is her mom. She is coming to her room. Sara is scared. She knows her mom will be angry. She hides the scissors and the dolls under her bed. She hopes her mom will not see them. She pretends to read a book. She tries to look good. Her mom opens the door and smiles. She says, "Hi, Sara. What are you doing?" Sara says, "I am reading a book, mom. It is a good book." Her mom says, "That's nice, Sara. Can I see your book?" Sara says, "Yes, mom. Here is my book." She gives her mom the book. Her mom looks at the book. She sees that it is upside down. She says, "Sara, why is your book upside down?" Sara says, "Oh, I did not know, mom. I am sorry, mom." Her mom says, "It's okay, Sara. But I think you need to learn how to read better. Maybe we can practice later. But first, can I see your dolls? I want to see how pretty they are." Sara says, "No, mom. You cannot see my dolls. They are sleeping. They are very tired. They need to rest." Her mom says, "Really? That's strange. I thought you liked to play with your dolls. Why are they sleeping?" Sara says, "They are sleeping because they are bored, mom. They have nothing to do. They have no fun." Her mom says, "That's sad, Sara. Maybe you can make them some fun. Maybe you can give them some new clothes or hair. Do you want to do that?" Sara says, "No, mom. I do not want to do that. I like their clothes and hair. They are very nice. They are very wild." Her mom says, "Wild? What do you mean, wild?" Sara says, "Nothing, mom. I mean, they are very happy. They are very wild and happy." Her mom says, "Hmm, I see. Well, I guess they are happy then. But I still want to see them. Can I please see them, Sara?" Sara says, "No, mom. You cannot see them. Please go away, mom. Please leave me alone, mom." Her mom says, "Sara, why are you being rude? Why are you hiding your dolls? What are you hiding, Sara?" Sara says, "Nothing, mom. I am hiding nothing. Please go away, mom. Please leave me alone, mom." Her mom says, "Sara, I am your mom. I love you. I want to help you. I want to see what you are hiding. Please show me, Sara. Please be honest, Sara." Sara says, "No, mom. I cannot show you. I cannot be honest. You will be mad, mom. You will be very mad." Her mom says, "Sara, I will not be mad. I will be understanding. I will be kind. Please trust me, Sara. Please show me, Sara." Sara says, "No, mom. I cannot trust you. I cannot show you. You will be mad, mom. You will be very mad." Her mom says, "Sara, please. Please, Sara. Please." Sara says, "No, mom. No, mom. No, mom."
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, in an ancient land, there was a boy named Tom. Tom liked to get up early and go to work with his dad. They went to a big place where they dug in the dirt to find old things. One day, while they were digging, Tom found a big box. He was very excited and called his dad over. They opened the box together and found a big, shiny rock. They didn't know what it was, but they knew it was special. Tom and his dad took the rock home to show mom. When they got home, mom looked at the rock and started to laugh. She said, "That's not a special rock, it's just a big, shiny ball!" Tom and his dad were surprised, but they all had a good laugh and played with the ball together.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a noisy bird. The bird liked to sing all the time. The bird had many friends who liked to listen to the bird sing. One day, the bird's friend, a cat, wanted to send a gift to another friend. The cat asked the bird to help. The bird said yes and took the gift to send it far away. The bird flew for a long time to send the gift. When the gift got to the friend, they were very happy. The bird sang a happy song and flew back home. The cat and the bird were happy they could help a friend.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a clumsy cat named Tom. Tom was always making a mess. One day, Tom wanted to work and help his friend, Sam the dog. Tom asked Sam, "Can I help you?" Sam said, "Yes, you can help me find my toy." They looked high and low for the toy. Tom was clumsy and knocked over a cup, but they kept looking. At last, they found the toy under a big tree. Tom and Sam were happy. They played together all day. Even though Tom was clumsy, he made Sam's life fun and full of love.
Write a short story about From.

Once upon a time, there was a big rhinoceros. He was very strong and had a big horn on his nose. He lived in a beautiful place with many trees and flowers. The rhinoceros liked to play with his friends, the birds and the monkeys. One day, while the rhinoceros was walking, he saw a little bird stuck in a tree. The little bird was very scared and was shouting for help. The rhinoceros wanted to help the little bird, but he didn't know how. The rhinoceros thought for a moment and then had an idea. He stood under the tree and used his big horn to gently push the tree. The tree moved and the little bird was free. The bird was very happy and thanked the rhinoceros for helping him. From that day on, the rhinoceros and the little bird became the best of friends.
Write a short story about Joe.

Joe was playing outside with his toys, when his mom called him over to the window. Shouts of, "Come and look Joe!" echoed throughout the room. He ran to the window, curious to see what his mom was on about. Out from the window, he admired a magnificent volcano. Its tall, pointed peak towered over the clouds, while its wide, fuming slopes fit perfectly into the landscape. "Look, Joe! Isn't it beautiful?" said his mom. With googly eyes, Joe nodded in agreement. "There is something truly special about this volcano," continued his mom. "It is fit and healthy, just like our family." Joe smiled as he continued to admire the volcano.
Write a short story about Timmy.

Once upon a time, there was a bright sun that would shine every day. In a small town, there lived a kind doctor. This doctor was always available to help people when they were sick. One day, a little boy named Timmy had a big problem. He was playing outside, and he fell down. Timmy hurt his knee and started to cry. His mom heard him and said, "Don't worry, Timmy. We will go see the doctor, and he will make you feel better." When they got to the doctor's office, the doctor looked at Timmy's knee. The doctor said, "I can fix your knee, but first, we need the sun to shine on it." Timmy was scared of the sun because it was so bright. But the doctor told him, "The sun is our friend. It will help your knee feel better." So, Timmy and the doctor went outside, and the sun shined on Timmy's knee. Timmy felt better right away. He said, "Thank you, doctor, and thank you, sun, for making my knee feel better." From that day on, Timmy was not scared of the sun anymore, and he knew that the doctor was always available to help him when he needed it.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She lived in a small house with her mom and dad. Lily liked to play outside, but today it was raining. She thought rain was boring because she could not go out to play. Lily's mom and dad were doing work in the house. They were cleaning and fixing things. Lily wanted to help, but she was too little. She felt sad because she could not do the work her mom and dad were doing. Then, Lily had an idea. She found a big box and put all her toys inside. She saved her toys from the rain by putting them in the box. When her mom and dad saw what she did, they were very happy. They said, "Good job, Lily! You saved your toys from the rain!" Lily felt proud and knew she could help, even if she was little.
Write a short story about Billy.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Billy. Billy loved to sing. Every day, he would fly around and sing a happy song. One day, while Billy was singing, he saw an orange on the ground. Billy flew down to the orange and said, "Hello, orange! I am Billy. Do you want to be friends?" The orange did not say anything. Billy was sad, but he kept singing. Suddenly, a fast squirrel named Sally ran by and picked up the orange. "Hi, Billy!" Sally said. "I heard you sing and I found my orange. Thank you!" Billy was happy that he helped Sally. From that day on, Billy and Sally were best friends, and they sang and played together every day.
Write a short story about Wednesday.

It was a very windy Wednesday. Becky and her friends decided to have a race. They hopped and skipped to the end of the street and lined up. "Ready, set, go!" said Dan excitedly. One by one they raced as fast as they could. But Becky was the fastest. Her feet moved quickly and she got to the end of the street first. "I won!" She yelled with glee. But then a bitter wind blew past them. Becky shuddered and the others pulled their jackets close. "Brrr," said the group, "Let's race again next Wednesday and stay warmer!"
Write a short story about Max.

One day, a dog named Max was chewing on his collar. His friend, a cat named Lily, saw him and said, "Why do you chew on your collar, Max?" Max replied, "I like how it feels on my teeth." Lily was jealous because she did not have a collar to chew on. Lily asked Max, "Can I chew on your collar too?" Max said, "No, it is mine. You can't chew on it." Lily was sad and walked away. She wanted a collar just like Max's. The next day, Lily found a collar under a tree. She was so happy! She put it on and started to chew it. Then, Max saw her and was surprised. He said, "Wow, you have a collar too now!" They both chewed on their collars and played together. Now, they were both happy and not jealous anymore.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a boy named Tom. Tom loved to play with his toys and his favorite toy was a flute. One day, Tom wanted to lift his flute high in the sky and play a song. He stood on his tippy-toes and lifted the flute as high as he could. As Tom played his flute, a big bird flew down from the sky. The bird looked dangerous with its sharp claws and big beak. Tom was scared, but the bird said, "Don't be scared, Tom. I like your flute song. Can you play it again?" Tom was surprised but happy. He played the flute again for the big bird. As he played, the dangerous bird started to dance. The bird was not mean, it just wanted to dance to Tom's flute song. Tom and the bird became friends and they danced together every day. And that's how Tom learned not to judge a book by its cover.
Write a short story about Why.

Once upon a time, there was a little butterfly. The butterfly was very pretty. It had big, colorful wings. The butterfly liked to fly around and play with its friends. One day, the butterfly was playing near a big tree. The tree had many branches. The butterfly saw a small bug on one of the branches. The bug looked sad. The butterfly asked the bug, "Why are you sad?" The bug said, "I did something bad, and now I am being punished. It is difficult for me to play." The butterfly wanted to help the bug. So, the butterfly talked to the bug's mom. The butterfly said, "I will help the bug be good. Please don't punish the bug anymore." The bug's mom agreed. The butterfly and the bug played together every day. They learned how to be good friends. The bug never did anything bad again. The bug was happy, and the butterfly was happy too. They lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a big, wide tree. It had many leaves and was very tall. A little boy named Tim loved to play near the tree every day. One day, Tim found a long rope under the tree. He had a fun idea. Tim asked his dad to help him tie the rope to a strong branch of the wide tree. His dad helped him, and they made a swing. Tim also found a big bottle near the tree. It was a bottle of wine that someone left there. Tim showed it to his dad. His dad took the wine away and said, "This is not for little kids." Tim didn't mind. He was happy to play on his new swing by the wide tree.
Write a short story about Toby.

Once upon a time, there were two brothers who were playing in their backyard. One brother, Toby, was running around and playing tag while the other brother, David, was playing with a toy boat in a nearby puddle. Suddenly, a big gust of wind came and pushed the boat far away into the pond. David ran to the edge of the pond and started to cry. Toby saw what happened and wanted to help. He came up with an idea. He took a long measure from his dad's shed and entered the pond. Gently, Toby pulled the boat near the edge of the pond and carefully handed it back to David. The two brothers shared a hug as David thanked Toby for his help. Toby smiled and said, "It's important to be gentle when helping others." Toby learned that it's better to give a hand than harsh words.
Write a short story about Mark.

Once upon a time, there was a big sea. In the sea, there lived a fish named Mark. Mark was a happy fish, but he was a bit ignorant. He didn't know much about the sea or other fish. One day, Mark met a wise old fish named Grandpa. Grandpa knew a lot about the sea. He told Mark many things about the sea, like where to find food and how to be safe. Mark listened and learned from Grandpa. Mark became smarter and happier. He swam in the sea with his new friend, Grandpa. They played and had fun together. Mark was not ignorant anymore. He knew a lot about the sea, and he was a happy fish.
Write a short story about Lily.

One day, a girl named Lily saw a fairy in her garden. The fairy was very small and had wings that sparkled. She was flying around the flowers and singing a lively song. "Hello, fairy!" Lily said. "What are you doing here?" The fairy smiled and landed on Lily's hand. "Hello, Lily!" she said. "I am looking for a place to set my home. I like your garden. It is very pretty and has many colors." Lily was happy to meet the fairy. She wanted to be her friend. "You can set your home here, fairy," she said. "I have a dollhouse that you can use. It is in my room. Do you want to see it?" The fairy nodded and flew with Lily to her room. Lily showed her the dollhouse. It had many rooms and furniture. The fairy liked it very much. She thanked Lily and set her home in the dollhouse. "Can I visit you sometimes, fairy?" Lily asked. "Of course, Lily!" the fairy said. "You are very kind and sweet. We can play and talk and have fun. You are my new friend." Lily and the fairy hugged and smiled. They were very happy. They had found each other in the garden.
Write a short story about No.

One day, a cat and a dog had a race. The cat was fast and the dog was slow. They wanted to see who could get to the cream first. The cream was in a big bowl, and it looked yummy. The cat said to the dog, "I bet I can get to the cream before you!" The dog said, "No, I will get there first!" So, they started to race. The cat ran fast, and the dog ran slow. The cat got to the cream first. But the cat saw that the dog was different and needed help. So, the cat waited for the dog. When the dog got there, they both shared the cream. They were happy and became friends.
Write a short story about Max.

One day, a helpful dog named Max found a hoop in the park. Max loved to play with hoops. He picked it up with his mouth and ran to find a friend to play with. Max saw a little girl named Lily. She was sad because she could not find her hoop. Max ran to Lily and gave her the hoop. Lily smiled and said, "Thank you, Max! You are very helpful." Lily and Max played with the hoop all day. They did not quit playing until the sun went down. Max was happy that he could help his new friend. And Lily was happy that she found her hoop and made a new friend too.
Write a short story about After.

One day, a boy found a dull notebook. The notebook was old and not pretty. The boy wanted to make the notebook nice again. He thought of a plan to make it pretty. The boy took the dull notebook to his room. He put nice colors on it. He used red, blue, and yellow. The notebook looked very pretty now. The boy was happy with his work. After some days, the boy saw the notebook rot. The colors were not nice anymore. The boy was sad. He learned that old things can rot. The boy decided to get a new notebook and make it pretty too.
Write a short story about Ella.

One day, a small girl called Ella asked her daddy to show her something special. After thinking for a bit, her daddy said he had a surprise - a mystery! Ella was so excited and asked him to show her. Her daddy reached into his pocket and took out a small box. He opened the box to reveal a small recording device inside. Ella was intrigued - she had never seen anything like it! Ella's daddy told her that this was a special recorder and it could record things like Ella's voice. Ella asked if she could try it and her daddy said yes. He showed her how to work it and she recorded her daddy's voice. The recording was so clear that Ella couldn't believe it. She had never heard anything so clear before! Ella and her daddy then listened to the recording together. Ella couldn't stop playing with the recorder - she had never seen anything so mysterious and magical before. Ella's daddy smiled and said that it was a perfect way to remember the special moment they had shared. Ella smiled and nodded in agreement - she was enjoying this special mystery!
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had a big, round face with a big smile. Lucy loved to play with her toys and have fun. One day, Lucy found an amazing toy in her toy box. It was a bright, shiny ball that could bounce very high. Lucy was so happy with her new toy. She played with it all day long. She would throw it up in the air and it would bounce back to her. But one day, while she was playing, the ball went very high and got stuck in a tree. Lucy was sad and didn't know what to do. Suddenly, a friendly bird flew by and saw Lucy's sad face. The bird wanted to help, so it flew up to the tree and tried to get the ball. The bird pushed the ball with its beak, but the ball wouldn't move. Then, the bird tried to scream loudly to scare the ball out of the tree. It worked! The ball fell down and Lucy caught it. She was so happy and thanked the bird with a big smile on her face. Lucy and the bird became good friends and played together every day.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, in a big world, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to climb. He would climb on chairs, tables, and even trees. One day, Tim saw a big, rough rock. He wanted to climb it. Tim started to climb the rough rock. He used his hands and feet to climb higher and higher. As he climbed, he saw many things. He saw birds, bugs, and even a squirrel. Tim was very happy. Finally, Tim reached the top of the rough rock. He looked around and saw the whole world. He felt like the king of the world. Tim waved to his mom and dad, who were watching him. They were proud of their brave little boy.
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily and Max were best friends. They liked to play in the park. They liked to swing, climb and run. But their favorite thing was the slide. The slide was big and shiny. It made them go fast. One day, they went to the park with their moms. Their moms said, "Be careful on the slide. Don't go too fast. Wait for your turn. And listen to us." Lily and Max were obedient. They nodded and said, "Yes, mom." They waited for their turn on the slide. They did not push or shout. They smiled and said, "Thank you" to the other kids. But when it was their turn, they had a naughty idea. They decided to go on the slide together. They thought it would be more fun. They thought they would go faster. They did not tell their moms. They climbed up the ladder. They sat on the slide. They held hands. They said, "Ready, set, go!" They pushed off. They speed down the slide. But they did not know that the slide was wet. It had rained the night before. The slide was slippery. They could not stop. They speed off the slide. They flew in the air. They landed on the grass. They rolled and tumbled. They got dirty and wet. They were not hurt. But they were scared. And they were sorry. They looked at their moms. Their moms looked at them. Their moms were not happy. They were angry. They ran to their moms. They hugged them. They said, "We're sorry, mom. We were bad. We did not listen. We won't do it again." Their moms hugged them back. They said, "We're glad you're okay. But you were naughty. You could have been hurt. You have to listen. You have to be careful. You have to be obedient." Lily and Max nodded. They said, "Yes, mom. We understand. We love you, mom." Their moms said, "We love you too. But now you have to go home. You have to take a bath. You have to change your clothes. And you have to say sorry to the other kids." Lily and Max said, "Okay, mom. We will do that." They said goodbye to the other kids. They said sorry to them. They got in their cars. They went home. They learned their lesson. They did not go on the slide together again. They were obedient. They were careful. They were happy.
Write a short story about Every.

Once upon a time, there was a green mailbox. It was near a big tree. Every day, a girl named Lily would walk to the mailbox. She would put a letter in it for her friend, Max. One day, Lily walked to the mailbox like she always did. But when she got there, she saw something strange. The green mailbox was open! There was a big hole in it. Lily was sad. She couldn't put her letter in the mailbox. Just then, Max came walking down the road. He saw the green mailbox and Lily. Max said, "I fixed the mailbox! I made it bigger for more letters." Lily was happy. Now she could send her letter to Max. They both walked home, smiling and laughing.
Write a short story about All.

Once upon a time, there was a bossy giant. He lived in a big house on a hill. All the people in the town were scared of him. One day, a little girl named Amy went up the hill to see the giant. She was not scared. Amy knocked on the giant's door. The giant opened the door and said, "Go away! I am busy!" But Amy did not go away. She said, "I want to be your friend." The giant was surprised. No one had ever wanted to be his friend before. The giant and Amy played together all day. They had so much fun! But then, it was time for Amy to go home. She said, "Goodbye, giant. I will come back tomorrow." The giant was sad, but he knew he had a new friend. He closed the door and smiled.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a calm boy named Tim went to the train station. He saw a big train with many cars. He was very happy because he loved trains. Tim got on the train with his mom and they sat down. As the train started to move, a man with a big hat came by. He said, "We are having a game. If you win, you get a big prize!" Tim wanted to play the game. The man gave Tim a ball and said, "Throw the ball and hit the cans. If you knock them all down, you win!" Tim threw the ball and knocked down all the cans. He won the game! The man with the big hat gave Tim a prize. It was a small train toy. Tim was so excited to play with his new toy. But then, something unexpected happened. The small train toy grew bigger and bigger! It became a real train! Everyone on the big train was surprised. Tim and his mom got off the big train and rode the new train all the way home. They had a fun and special day.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She loved to eat fruits. One day, she saw a big, purple grape. She had never seen a grape before. Lily was very happy. Lily wanted to examine the grape. She looked at it very closely. She saw that it was round and shiny. It looked yummy. Lily touched the grape. It was soft and a little bit wet. Lily put the grape in her mouth. She took a big bite. It tasted sweet and a little bit sour. She liked it a lot. Lily ate all the grapes she could find. She was very full and happy.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a unique cat named Tom. Tom loved to play with paper. One day, Tom found a long, colorful paper. Tom had an idea. He said to his friend, the dog, "Let's tie the paper to the tree and play!" The dog wagged his tail and said, "Yes, let's do that!" They tied the paper to the tree and played all day. They ran and jumped and had so much fun. At the end of the day, the paper flew away. But they knew they could find more paper and play again tomorrow.
Write a short story about Every.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big hill. The hill was very high. Every day, the sun would rise and shine on the town. One day, a little boy named Tim found a bottle. The bottle had poison in it. Tim knew that poison was bad, so he wanted to get rid of it. Tim thought that the high hill was the best place to throw the poison away. So, he walked up the hill as the sun began to rise. When he reached the top, he threw the poison far away. The town was safe, and Tim felt happy.
Write a short story about Bad.

Once upon a time, there was a big, ugly cat. This cat liked to poke things with its paw. One day, the cat saw a cup of milk on the table. The cat jumped on the table and poked the cup of milk. The milk spilled all over the table. The cat was happy and licked the milk. The cat's owner saw the mess and said, "Bad cat! No more milk for you." The ugly cat learned not to poke things and lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Max.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a little dog named Max. Max loved to chew on things. He would chew on toys, shoes, and even sticks. One day, Max found a razor in the bathroom. He didn't know what it was, but he thought it looked fun to chew on. Max started to chew on the razor, but it hurt his mouth. He yelped and dropped it on the floor. His owner, a kind little girl named Lily, heard the noise and came running into the bathroom. She saw Max and the razor on the floor, and she knew he had made a mistake. Lily felt sad that Max had hurt himself. Max felt ashamed for chewing on something he shouldn't have. He looked at Lily with his big, sad eyes. Lily hugged Max and told him it was okay. She knew Max didn't mean to do anything wrong. From that day on, Max promised to only chew on his toys, and he never went near the razor again.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a man named Tim. Tim liked to drive his big car. One day, he put on his nice suit and went for a drive. As Tim drove, he saw a small, fragile bird on the side of the road. The bird looked sad. Tim stopped his car and got out. He walked over to the bird and said, "Hello, little bird. Why are you sad?" The bird looked up at Tim and said, "I am lost. Can you help me find my way home?" Tim smiled and said, "Of course, little bird. Hop in my car and we will find your home together." So, the bird got into Tim's car and they drove around, looking for the bird's home. After a while, they found it. The bird was so happy and thanked Tim for his help. Tim waved goodbye to the bird and drove back home, feeling happy that he could help a friend in need.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a big red ball that she loved to play with. One day, the big red ball rolled away and got stuck in a tree. Lily was very sad. A kind man named Tom saw Lily and wanted to help. He had a long chain that he thought could get the ball down. He threw the chain up and it pulled the ball out of the tree. The ball fell down and Lily was so happy. Lily received her ball back and was very grateful to Tom. She gave him a big hug to say thank you. Now, Lily and Tom are good friends and they play with the big red ball together.
Write a short story about Sue.

One day, a little girl named Sue woke up in her room. She saw something funny on the floor. It was a big, red ball. Sue did not know how the ball got there, but she wanted to play with it. Sue kicked the ball and it went out of her room. She followed the ball and saw it roll into the kitchen. But something unexpected happened. The ball turned into a big, red cat! Sue was so surprised that she laughed. The big, red cat was funny and nice. Sue played with the cat all day. They had so much fun together. At night, the cat turned back into a ball. Sue took the ball to her room and went to sleep. She could not wait to wake up and play with her new friend again.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was an adorable frog. The frog lived in a swamp. The frog had a job to deliver things to his friends. One day, the frog had to deliver a big box. He did not know what was inside. He hopped and hopped to his friend's house. On the way, the box opened, and out came a big, scary monster! But the monster was not mean. He was nice! The monster and the frog became friends. They went to the swamp together and played. The frog and the monster had fun. They were happy.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a little boy named Tim and his dog, Spot, went for a walk. They saw a big pipe with water coming out. It was a leak! Tim said, "Oh no, we need to stop the leak!" Tim and Spot ran to find help. They saw a big, dangerous lion. The lion roared, "Why are you here?" Tim was scared, but he said, "There is a leak, and we need to stop it. Can you help us?" The lion nodded and followed Tim and Spot. When they got to the leak, the lion used his big paw to stop the water. The leak was fixed! But then, the lion smiled and said, "Thank you for letting me help. I am not dangerous, I just wanted to be friends." Tim and Spot were happy, and they all became good friends.
Write a short story about Lily.

One day, a curious cat named Lily saw a big box. The box had a pretty wrap on it. Lily wanted to know what was inside the box. She tried to open it, but she could not. She saw a spike near the box. Lily thought the spike could help her open the box. Lily called her friend, a dog named Max. "Max, can you help me open the box?" she asked. Max came and tried to use the spike to open the box. But the spike was stuck! Max and Lily pulled and pulled, but they could not get the spike out. Then, a wise old owl named Oliver saw them. "Why are you fighting with the spike?" he asked. Max and Lily told him about the box. Oliver said, "You do not need the spike. Just use your paws!" Max and Lily tried to open the box with their paws. With a little help from Oliver, they opened the box. Inside, they found a big, soft blanket. They were so happy and shared the blanket with their friends.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time there was a small boy named Tom. Tom was feeling very sad, so his mom hugged him and comforted him. Tom soon felt a bit better and began to explore his garden. In the corner of the garden, Tom noticed a very rare flower. It was a beautiful flower and was releasing steam! Tom was so excited to see the steam. Tom watched the steam float up into the sky and as it did, he felt a warm and comforting feeling inside him. It was like the steam was giving him a message! The next day, Tom went back to the garden and the steam was still there and so was the rare flower. Tom realized that when he watches the steam, he feels happy! From then on, every time Tom was feeling sad, he would go to the garden and watch the steam. The steam would comfort him and he would feel happy again.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a boy named Tim saw a big cloud in the sky. He went to his mom and said, "Look, mom! Big cloud!" His mom looked and saw it too. She knew a tornado might come, so she got ready. Tim and his mom went to find a safe place. They looked for an available room in their house. They found a small room with no windows. Tim's mom said, "We will stay here until the tornado goes away." They waited in the small room. Tim played with his toys and his mom read a book. Soon, the tornado was gone. They went outside and saw that everything was okay. Tim's mom said, "Good job, Tim! You helped us stay safe!"
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a pretty blouse with flowers on it. One day, she went to the park to play with her friends. The park was big and had many trees. While playing, Lily saw a little bird in a cage. The bird looked sad and wanted to escape. Lily said, "I will help you, little bird!" She opened the cage and the bird flew away. The bird was so happy to be free. The bird said, "Thank you, Lily! You are very kind." Lily smiled and felt happy too. She looked at her blouse and saw that the pocket was empty. She said, "That's okay, I'm glad I could help the bird." And they all played together in the park until it was time to go home.
Write a short story about Max.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Max. Max was a loyal dog who loved to play. He had a favorite toy, a red ball with a top that would spin when Max pushed it. One day, Max and his best friend, a little girl named Lily, went to the park to play. They ran and played with the red ball, and Max would spin the top with his nose. They laughed and had so much fun. As the sun began to settle, Max and Lily knew it was time to go home. Max carried the red ball in his mouth, and Lily held his leash. They walked back home, happy and tired from their day at the park. Max and Lily were best friends, and they knew they would always be loyal to each other.
Write a short story about Sue.

Once upon a time, in a cheerful little town, there was a girl named Sue. Sue loved to make things with her hands. She would stitch and sew all day long. One day, she decided to make a special gift for her friend Tom. Sue worked very hard to make a beautiful toy for Tom. She stitched and sewed with bright colors. As she worked, she said to herself, "Tom will be so happy when he sees this toy. I can't wait to give it to him!" The next day, Sue met Tom at the park. She hid the toy behind her back and said, "Tom, I have a surprise for you!" Tom was excited and curious. He knew Sue had been working on something special. Sue showed Tom the toy she had made. Tom's eyes lit up, and he said, "Wow, Sue! This is the best gift ever! Thank you so much!" Sue was so happy that she had made her friend smile. From that day on, Sue and Tom played with the toy every day. They had lots of fun together, and their friendship grew stronger. The toy that Sue had made brought them closer, and they lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Inside.

Once upon a time, there was a big ship. The ship had an open door. Inside the ship, there were many animals. They all liked to play and have fun. One day, the animals decided to have a dance party. They invited all their friends to come and dance on the ship. The music was loud and happy. The animals danced and danced. They jumped and clapped their hands. As the sun went down, the animals were tired but happy. They said goodbye to their friends and went to sleep in the open ship. They all had sweet dreams about their fun dance party. The end.
Write a short story about Emma.

It was a fair sunny day, and three year old Emma was ready for her jog. She tied her sneakers tightly and started running. After going around the block twice, she felt tired and decided to take a quick break. Her friend Tom had arrived at the park and asked her to play tag with him. Emma wanted to join, but she knew she had to stay focused on her jog. She gently said no; she couldn't accept his invitation, but she wanted to. However, Tom kept asking and Emma felt annoyed. She wanted to have some fun, but her jog was important too. Tom kept insisting, so Emma decided to compromise. She said she would play tag with him, but only after she finished jogging. They agreed, and Tom waited. Finally, when Emma was done with her jog, they began playing tag. They had a lot of fun, and they both felt happy afterwards. Emma was glad she accepted Tom's invitation; she was proud that she had been fair with herself and stuck to her goal first.
Write a short story about Kitty.

One day, a clever cat named Kitty went to a lecture. The lecture was about how to fold paper. Kitty sat and listened carefully. Kitty said, "I want to fold paper too!" So, she tried to fold a paper boat. She folded and folded, but it was not easy. Her friend, Doggy, came to help her. Doggy said, "Let me try!" Together, they folded the paper boat. In the end, Kitty and Doggy were very happy with their paper boat. They put it in a small pond and watched it float. Their friends saw the boat and clapped. Kitty and Doggy were proud of their clever work.
Write a short story about Lila.

Lila and Ben are friends who like to play with blocks. They build towers and houses and cars with the blocks. One day, they decide to make a big tower with all the blocks they have. They work hard and stack the blocks high and wide. They are very proud of their tower. "Look at our tower!" Lila says. "It is so big and original. No one has a tower like ours." "Yes, it is amazing!" Ben says. "We are the best builders ever. Do you think we can mail our tower to someone?" Lila thinks for a moment. She likes the idea of sharing their tower with someone else. But she also likes their tower and does not want to lose it. "Who do you want to mail it to?" she asks. Ben thinks too. He remembers his grandma, who lives far away and sends him letters and stickers sometimes. He loves his grandma and misses her. "Maybe we can mail it to my grandma," he says. "She would like our tower. She likes colorful things and she always says we are creative." Lila nods. She likes Ben's grandma too. She is very nice and tells funny stories. She thinks she would like their tower too. "Okay, let's mail it to your grandma," she says. "But how do we do that?" Ben looks around. He sees a big cardboard box in the corner. He has an idea. "Maybe we can put our tower in that box," he says. "And then we can write a letter and draw a picture and put them in the box too. And then we can ask mom or dad to take the box to the mail place." Lila likes Ben's idea. She thinks it is clever and kind. "Let's do that," she says. "But first, let's take a photo of our tower. So we can remember it and show it to our friends." Ben agrees. He finds his mom's phone and takes a photo of their tower. They smile and hug their tower. Then they carefully take apart their tower and put the blocks in the box. They also write a letter and draw a picture for Ben's grandma. They tape the box and write "To Grandma" on it. They are ready to mail their tower. They go to find mom or dad and show them their box. Mom and dad are surprised and impressed by their tower and their letter and their picture. They praise them for their work and their generosity. They agree to take the box to the mail place. Lila and Ben are happy. They hope their grandma will like their tower. They also hope she will write back and send them a photo of her with their tower. They think they have done a good thing. They hug each other and go to play with some other toys. They are still the best builders ever.
Write a short story about Do.

One day, a small leaf was in a big tree. The leaf was not fit. It was sad because it could not play with the other leaves. The wind came and said, "Do you want to escape and see the world?" "Yes, please," said the small leaf. The wind helped the leaf escape from the tree. They went on a fun trip. They saw a big hill, a small dog, and a pretty flower. The small leaf was happy. At the end of the day, the small leaf said, "Thank you, wind, for helping me escape. I learned that even if I am not fit, I can still have fun and see the world." The wind smiled and took the leaf back to the tree. The other leaves saw the small leaf was happy and they all played together. The moral of the story is that everyone is special and can have fun, even if they are not fit.
Write a short story about Many.

Once upon a time, there was a lonely fish in a big pond. The fish was sad because he had no friends. One day, he saw a long rod in the water. The rod was alone too, so the fish swam close to it. Then, the fish saw a little boy holding the rod. The boy looked sad and lonely too. The fish wanted to be friends with the boy. So, the fish decided to teach the boy how to play with him. The fish jumped out of the water and the boy laughed. The boy and the fish became friends. They played together every day. The fish was not lonely anymore. But one day, the boy did not come. The fish waited and waited, but the boy never came back. The fish felt lonely again. But then, something unexpected happened. Many new fish friends came to the pond to play with the lonely fish. They all lived happily together, and the lonely fish was not lonely anymore.
Write a short story about Tim.

Tim and Mia are friends. They like to play outside. Tim has a cool scooter. It is red and shiny. Mia wants to try it. "Can I get your scooter, Tim?" Mia asks. "OK, but be careful. It is fast and fun," Tim says. Mia gets on the scooter. She pushes with her foot. The scooter goes zoom! Mia feels happy. She likes the wind in her hair. "Wow, this is cool!" Mia says. Tim smiles. He is glad Mia likes his scooter. He runs after her. "Wait for me, Mia!" Tim says. They play with the scooter until it is time to go home. They share the scooter and take turns. They are good friends. They have fun.
Write a short story about Sally.

One day, a little girl named Sally went for a walk. She saw an open mailbox on the street. She looked inside and found a small bottle. The bottle had a note that said, "Drink me, and you will shrink." Sally was curious, so she drank from the bottle. She started to feel funny, and then she began to shrink! Soon, she was as small as a bug. Sally said, "Oh no! I'm so tiny! How will I get big again?" Sally remembered that the note said she would shrink, but it didn't say how to get big again. She decided to go back to the open mailbox to see if there was another note. When she got there, she found a new note that said, "Eat the cookie, and you will grow." Sally ate the cookie and started to grow back to her normal size. Sally was so happy to be big again. She learned that it's important to be careful and not to use things she doesn't know about. From that day on, she never touched anything in an open mailbox again.
Write a short story about Mia.

One day, a little girl named Mia found a notebook. She was very hungry, so she thought maybe the notebook could help her find food. Mia opened the notebook and saw words she did not know. She asked her mom to explain the words. "Mom, can you explain these words to me?" Mia said. Her mom looked at the notebook and said, "These words tell us how to make a yummy cake. We can make it together!" But, Mia's brother Max was not happy. He wanted to play with the notebook. Max tried to take the notebook from Mia. "No, Max!" Mia said, "We need it to make a cake!" Mia's mom helped them solve the problem. She said, "Max, you can help us make the cake too! And after we eat, you can play with the notebook." Max agreed, and they all made the cake together. They were not hungry anymore, and Max got to play with the notebook.
Write a short story about Maybe.

Once upon a time, there was a big, blue armchair. The armchair was sad because no one would sit on it. It wanted to make friends and have someone to settle on it. The armchair was in a room with many toys, but the toys never played with it. One day, a little boy came into the room. The armchair was very happy and hoped the boy would sit on it. The boy looked at the armchair and smiled. The armchair thought, "Maybe he will settle on me and we can be friends." But then, the boy saw a ball and started to play with it. He did not sit on the armchair. The armchair was very sad again. It thought, "Why doesn't anyone want to sit on me?" The armchair stayed sad and alone, waiting for someone to be its friend. But no one ever did.
Write a short story about Sally.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sally. She had a big print of a sweet cake. She wanted to sell the print to buy a real sweet cake for herself. Sally went to the park to find someone to buy her print. At the park, Sally saw a boy named Tom. She asked him, "Do you want to buy my sweet cake print? I want to sell it!" Tom looked at the print and said, "Yes, I will buy it!" Sally was very happy. Sally gave the print to Tom and took the money. She ran to the shop to buy a sweet cake. But when she got there, the shop was closed. Sally was very sad. She could not buy the sweet cake she wanted.
Write a short story about Mia.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia loved to play with her toys. One day, Mia saw a light, pretty butterfly in her room. It was on top of her toy box. Mia wanted to catch the butterfly, but she could not reach it. "Mom, help me!" Mia said. "I want the butterfly, but I can't reach it." Mom came into the room and saw the butterfly. She gave Mia a wink and said, "Let's think of a way to reach it together." Mom and Mia put a chair near the toy box. Mia stood on the chair and reached up. Now, Mia could reach the butterfly. Mia was so happy, she said, "Thank you, Mom!" They both smiled and played with the butterfly. The butterfly flew around the room, making Mia and Mom laugh.
Write a short story about Jack.

Once upon a time, there was a lively little boy named Jack. Jack was always eager to explore and one day he followed a number to a mysterious place. It lead him to a meadow full of bright colors and animals. He smiled when he saw them. When he got closer, one of the animals said to him in a sweet voice, "What brings you here to this meadow, Jack?" Jack was surprised to hear the animal today, but he replyed confidently, "This number lead me here!" The animal nodded knowingly and said, "Ah, the number! Follow it, stay lively and look closely. You may find something special". Jack thought this strange but he agreed. As Jack followed the number, he saw more colors and animals. Then, eventually, he came to a sparkly pond. When he looked at it closely, he was amazed to find colorful frogs jumping up and down in the water. Jack's eyes lit up with wonder. He was so happy the number had lead him there!
Write a short story about Max.

Once upon a time, there was a big, heavy cat named Max. Max loved to eat. One day, Max smelled something good coming from the oven. Max's mom was baking a cake. Max wanted to eat the cake, but he knew he had to wait. Max tried to be patient, but the cake smelled so good. Max couldn't wait any longer. He jumped up and opened the oven. The cake was still hot, but Max didn't care. He took a big bite. Max's mom saw him eating the cake and was sad. Max felt bad for eating the cake. He said, "I'm sorry, Mom." Max's mom forgave him and said, "It's okay, Max. Just remember to wait next time." Max promised to wait and they both shared the rest of the cake.
Write a short story about Elly.

Once upon a time, there was a big elephant named Elly. Elly loved to play with her trumpet. She would blow it loud and strong. It made a fun sound that made her friends dance. One day, Elly went to the park to play. She saw a rough tree and decided to rub her back on it. As she was rubbing, her trumpet fell out of her bag. A little mouse saw the trumpet and picked it up. The mouse tried to blow the trumpet, but the sound was too low. Elly heard the sound and went to see who was playing her trumpet. She saw the little mouse and said, "Please, give me back my trumpet!" The mouse looked sad and said, "I just want to play like you." Elly thought for a moment and had an idea. She asked the mouse to repeat what she did. Elly blew her trumpet, and the mouse copied her. They played together, and the sound was perfect. The mouse and Elly became best friends, and they played music every day. And that's how Elly learned that even the smallest friend can make a big sound.
Write a short story about Ralph.

Once upon a time there was a cute, little rabbit named Ralph. He lived all alone in the woods and here he searched for food every day. One day, Ralph heard a strange noise coming from a bush nearby. He peeped in and saw a tiny kitten struggling to get out. "Miaow!" said the kitten. Ralph hopped up to the kitten and said, "Don't worry, I'll help!" He grabbed the kitten's soft paw with his own, and pulled. The rabbit pulled with all his might, struggling to free the kitten. Ralph must have done something right because seconds later the kitten was free. The kitten was so thankful, and she licked Ralph on the chin as a sign of thanks. The rabbit and the kitten shared a few more seconds of joy until the kitten ran away. Ralph looked back one last time at the kitten and smiled. That was one of the best days he had ever had, and the rabbit had a warm, fuzzy feeling inside!
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. He loved to play outside in the snow. He would raise his arms high and let the snowflakes fall on his face. Tim thought the snow was delicious when he tasted it on his tongue. One day, Tim saw a big snow hill. He wanted to climb it, but his friend, Sam, said he was too small. Tim felt sad, but he did not give up. He tried to climb the hill, but he slipped and fell. He tried again, and this time, he made it to the top. Sam was surprised and said sorry to Tim. They played together on the snow hill. The moral of the story is to never give up, even when others say you can't do something. Just like Tim, you can do anything if you try hard enough. And always be kind to your friends, even if they make mistakes.
Write a short story about Buddy.

Once upon a time, in a small house, there lived a brown dog named Buddy. Buddy loved to play with his toys and run around the house. The house had lots of furniture for Buddy to jump on and hide under. One day, Buddy's family had a big party. Lots of people came to the house to watch Buddy perform tricks. Buddy could sit, stay, and roll over. Everyone clapped and cheered when Buddy performed his tricks. After the party, Buddy was tired. He went to his favorite brown chair and lay down. Buddy's family was so proud of him for performing his tricks. They gave him a big hug and a special treat. Buddy fell asleep, happy and loved.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a boy named Tim went to play with his toy train. He liked the train a lot. It was wide and could go very fast. Tim played with the train all day by himself. The next day, Tim saw a girl named Sue. Sue wanted to play with the train too. Tim thought about it and said, "We can share my train and play together." Sue was very happy. Tim and Sue played with the train. But then, something unexpected happened. The train started to move by itself! It went very fast and took Tim and Sue on a fun ride. They laughed and said, "This is the best train ever!"
Write a short story about Bob.

Once upon a time, there was a quiet fireman named Bob. Bob was very good at his job. He loved to help people and put out fires. One day, Bob was walking to work when he saw a big red balloon. "Wow! Look at that big balloon!" said a little girl named Sue. She was standing next to Bob. "I wish I could soar up to the sky like that balloon." Just then, something unexpected happened. A gust of wind blew the balloon right into Bob's hands! Bob looked at Sue and smiled. "Would you like to soar with me?" he asked. Sue nodded her head, and they both held onto the balloon. It lifted them up into the sky, and they soared above the town. Everyone looked up and waved at Bob and Sue. They had a great time flying high and seeing the world from above. When they were done, Bob and Sue landed safely on the ground, and they both knew it was a day they would never forget.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Lily. She had a new veil that her mom gave her. Lily loved her new veil so much. She wore it every day. One day, while playing in the park, Lily met a boy named Tim. Tim saw the new veil and said, "Lily, your veil is pretty!" Lily was happy, but she ignored Tim because she wanted to play alone. Later, Lily saw Tim playing with other kids. She felt sad and wanted to join them. She went to Tim and said, "Can I play with you?" Tim smiled and said, "Yes, Lily! Let's play together!" From that day on, Lily and Tim became good friends. They played together every day. Lily learned that it's important not to ignore others and to share her happiness with friends. The moral of the story is: Don't ignore others; we should be kind and share our happiness with everyone.
Write a short story about Spot.

Once upon a time, there was a little dog named Spot. Spot was a very obedient dog. He always did what his owner, a kind man named Tom, told him to do. Tom and Spot loved to play together and go for walks in the park. One day, while they were walking, Spot saw a ball. He recognized the ball as his own, and he ran after it. The ball rolled into the woods, and Spot followed it. Tom called out, "Spot, come back!" But Spot did not hear him. After some time, Spot found the ball and picked it up. He heard Tom calling for him, so he ran back to where Tom was waiting. Tom was happy to see Spot and said, "Good boy, Spot! Thank you for finding your ball and coming back to me." Spot wagged his tail, proud of his work. From that day on, Spot always stayed close to Tom during their walks.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a polite cat named Tim. Tim liked to help everyone. One day, Tim saw a little boy who was lost. Tim wanted to help the boy find his mom. Tim took the boy to the police. The police help people who are lost. The police officer was happy to see Tim and the boy. The officer said, "Thank you, Tim, for bringing the boy to us." Tim felt good for helping the boy. The police officer warned Tim and the boy to be careful when they walk in the town. He told them to hold hands so they don't get lost again. Tim and the boy listened to the officer and walked home safely.
Write a short story about Now.

One day, a little insect was in the garden. He liked to listen to the birds sing. The insect had a friend, a bossy ant. The bossy ant always told the insect what to do. One sunny day, the bossy ant told the insect, "You must help me carry food." The insect did not want to help, but he did it anyway. They walked and walked, carrying food together. They were very tired. Then, something unexpected happened. A big wind came and took the food away. The bossy ant was sad, but the insect was happy. He said, "Now we can listen to the birds again!" The bossy ant smiled and they both listened to the birds sing. The bossy ant learned to have fun and not be so bossy anymore.
Write a short story about Sue.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue had a favorite toy, a rock. She loved to play with her rock. She would throw it, roll it, and even sleep with it. One day, Sue found a big cup in the kitchen. She had an idea. She took her rock and put it in the cup. Sue shook the cup, and the rock made a fun sound. She danced around the room, happy and alert. Sue showed her mom the rock in the cup. Her mom smiled and danced with her. They both had lots of fun with the rock and the cup. Sue knew she would always remember this special day.
Write a short story about What.

Once upon a time, there was a big, wide lake. On one side of the lake lived a family of ducks. They liked to swim in the lake and play in the sunshine. But one day, they noticed something strange. A big, wide skull was floating in the lake. The ducks were scared, so they decided to find out what it was. They asked their friend, the fish, and a wise old frog. What could it be? The fish said it could be the skull of a monster. The wise old frog imagined it was the skull of a witch. The ducks didn't know what to do. So, they asked a brave little bird. She looked at the skull and said it was nothing to worry about. It was just the old skull of a huge, wide turtle. The ducks were relieved. From that day on, they asked the brave bird for help when they didn't know what to do. And they all lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Suddenly.

Once upon a time, there was a little, red bird. The bird lived in a big tree near a house. The house had a bathroom with a big window. The bird liked to look inside the bathroom through the window. One day, the bird saw the water in the bathroom increase. The water was going up and up! The bird knew this was not good. The bird flew to the house to tell the people inside. When the bird got to the door, it saw a big, red button. The bird pushed the button with its beak. Suddenly, the water stopped going up in the bathroom. The bird had saved the day! The people inside the house were so happy and thanked the little, red bird.
Write a short story about Lily.

One day, a little girl named Lily found a dead bug. She felt sad for the bug. Lily wanted to do something nice for it. She thought, "I will write a poem for the bug!" Lily sat under a tree and started to write her poem. She thought about how the bug would spring and play when it was alive. Lily smiled and finished her poem. She read it out loud, "Little bug, you used to spring, now you rest with a wing. I hope you fly high, up in the sky." Lily's friend, Tim, heard her poem and came over. He said, "That's a nice poem, Lily. I like how you talked about the bug springing and playing." Lily smiled and said, "Thank you, Tim. I wanted to do something nice for the bug." They both looked at the dead bug and felt happy that they could remember it with a beautiful poem.
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. She had an adorable cat named Mittens. They were best friends and played together all the time. One day, Lucy found a dirty rag on the ground. She picked it up and started to play with it. Mittens didn't like the dirty rag and wanted to play with her clean toys. So, Mittens tried to slap the rag out of Lucy's hand. Lucy didn't understand why Mittens didn't want to play with the rag. Lucy's mom saw them playing and came to help. She explained to Lucy that the rag was dirty and not good to play with. She showed Lucy how to clean the rag and make it nice again. Lucy and Mittens learned that it's important to keep things clean and to take care of their toys.
Write a short story about Red.

One day, a bossy car named Red was driving down the road. Red liked to go very fast. He used a lot of fuel when he drove. He did not like to share the road with other cars. Red would honk and say, "Get out of my way! I am the fastest car!" One day, Red met a small, slow car named Blue. Blue was very kind and always shared the road. Red was so bossy and said to Blue, "You are too slow! You should not be on the road!" Blue felt sad but did not want to fight with Red. Blue said, "We can both use the road. It is for everyone." As days went by, Red kept using a lot of fuel and driving too fast. One day, Red was going so fast that he could not stop. He crashed into a big wall and destroyed his engine. Blue saw what happened and went to help Red. Blue shared his fuel and helped Red fix his engine. Red learned that being bossy and using too much fuel was not good. He also learned that sharing and being kind was important. From that day on, Red and Blue became good friends and shared the road with everyone. The moral of the story is that it is better to be kind and share than to be bossy and selfish.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, there was a man named Tom. Tom liked to work hard. He had a big yellow helmet. He wore it every day when he went to work. Tom's job was to build tall houses. One sunny day, Tom went to work. He put on his yellow helmet. He saw that his friends were there too. They all had their own helmets. They all worked hard together. They had fun and laughed while they built the tall houses. After a long day, Tom and his friends were tired. They had worked very hard. But they were happy because they had built a beautiful tall house. They took off their helmets and went home to rest. The next day, they would put on their helmets again and work hard to build more tall houses.
Write a short story about Let.

One day, a big cat and a little dog went to the store. They wanted to buy a ball to play with. The big cat said, "I will pay for the ball." The little dog was very happy and gave the big cat a wink. They played with the ball in the park. The big cat was very alert. He saw a big tree and said, "Let's throw the ball and see who can catch it first." The little dog was excited and agreed. The big cat threw the ball very high. The little dog ran as fast as he could. He remembered the big cat's wink and was alert. The ball went behind the big tree. The little dog caught the ball and won. The big cat smiled and said, "Good job, little dog! You were very alert and fast!" The little dog was very proud and happy.
Write a short story about Sue.

One day, a girl named Sue went for a walk. She walked to the west. The sun was warm and the sky was blue. Sue saw a big tree. Under the tree, she found a tidy little house. Sue knocked on the door.
