He did not listen to his mom or his dad when they asked him to do something. He only wanted to watch videos. One day, Sam's tablet ran out of power. He tried to plug it in, but it did not work. He looked for another plug, but he could not find one. He was very sad and angry. He cried and shouted. He did not know what to do without his videos. His mom came to see what was wrong. She saw Sam's tablet and his tears. She felt sorry for him, but she also wanted to teach him a lesson. She said, "Sam, you watch too many videos. You need to do other things, like play, read, and help. Videos are fun, but they are not everything. You need to balance your time and your activities. Do you understand?" Sam nodded, but he still felt unhappy. He did not like to hear his mom's words. He felt uncomfortable. He wanted his videos back. He said, "But mom, I love videos. They make me happy. Can I have my tablet back, please?" His mom said, "No, Sam. Not today. Today, you will do something else. You will help me dry the dishes. It is easy and fun. You will see." She took Sam to the kitchen and gave him a towel. She showed him how to dry the dishes and put them away. She smiled and praised him when he did a good job. Sam did not like it at first. He thought it was boring and hard. He missed his videos. But as he dried the dishes, he started to enjoy it. He liked to see the dishes shine. He liked to hear his mom's voice. He liked to feel useful and proud. He realized that drying the dishes was not so bad. It was actually fun. He finished drying the dishes and hugged his mom. He said, "Thank you, mom. I'm sorry I was rude. I learned my lesson. I will not watch videos all day. I will do other things, too. I will play, read, and help. I will balance my time and my activities. I understand now." His mom hugged him back and said, "I'm proud of you, Sam. You are a good boy. You learned a valuable lesson. Videos are fun, but they are not everything. There is more to life than videos. You need to explore, learn, and grow. You need to be happy and healthy. You need to be yourself." Sam smiled and said, "I love you, mom. You are the best. Can I watch one video now?" His mom laughed and said, "Okay, Sam. Just one. But only after you eat your lunch. Deal?" Sam agreed and said, "Deal. Thank you, mom. You are the best." The end.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a pale little boy named Tim. He liked to play outside in the sun. One day, he went for a walk in the woods near his home. As Tim walked, he saw some ash on the ground. He thought it was strange, but he kept going. He took a big step over the ash and kept walking. The woods were very quiet, and Tim felt a little scared. Soon, the sky began to get dark. Tim knew he had to go home, but he was lost. He tried to find his way back, but he couldn't. Tim started to cry. He sat down on the ground and waited for someone to find him. But no one came. The woods got darker and darker, and Tim was all alone.
Write a short story about All.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a big storm. The wind blew hard and the rain fell down. All the people in the town stayed in their homes, safe and warm. The next day, the sun came out, but the town was very messy. Leaves, sticks, and mud were everywhere. A little girl named Lily saw the mess and decided to help. She took her broom and started to clean. Soon, her friends saw her and they wanted to help too. Together, they cleaned the town, making it nice and neat again. The people were happy and thanked the children for their hard work. The children learned that when they work together, they can make a big difference. And that was the moral of the story: helping each other makes everything better.
Write a short story about Farmer.

Once upon a time, there was a small village filled with people who worked very hard. They grew many different crops like wheat, which Farmer John took very seriously. One day, Farmer John was walking around his wheat fields, when he heard something coming from one of the fields. He went over to have a look, and he found three small children rolling around in the wheat. When he asked what they were doing, one of the children spoke up. “We’re filling the wheat with lots of love, so that everyone can share it!” said the child, with a smile. Farmer John couldn’t help but smile too. He knew that even though the children were being silly, it was still a very serious thing they were doing. He gave the children a big hug, thanked them, and then sent them on their way. From that day on, Farmer John made sure the wheat he grew was filled with lots of love, just like the children said.
Write a short story about Bob.

Once upon a time, there was a big bull named Bob. Bob lived on a farm with many friends. They played all day and had fun. One day, Bob's friend, a small bird named Betty, lost her toy. She was very sad. Bob wanted to help, so he said, "Betty, let's play a game. I will guess where your toy is and find it for you." Betty was happy and said, "Okay, Bob! Let's play." Bob started to guess where the toy could be. "Is it in the barn?" he asked. "No," said Betty. "Is it by the pond?" he asked again. "Yes!" said Betty, excited. They went to the pond and found the toy. Betty was very grateful and thanked Bob. They played with the toy and had a great day together.
Write a short story about No.

Once upon a time, there was a huge tree. It had many big branches. On one branch, a sandwich was hanging. No one knew how it got there. A little girl named Amy saw the sandwich. She wanted to eat it. But she was too small to reach the branch. She thought and thought of a way to get the sandwich. Amy had an idea. She asked her friend, a tall boy named Tom, for help. Tom reached up and got the sandwich. He gave it to Amy. They both shared the sandwich and were very happy.
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily liked to organize her toys. She had a big box for her dolls, a small box for her cars, and a basket for her books. She put each toy in the right place and made her room look neat. One day, she found an unknown toy on the floor. It was round and shiny and had many buttons. She did not know what it was or where it came from. She wondered if it was a gift from someone or if it was a mistake. She picked up the toy and pressed a button. The toy made a loud noise and flashed a bright light. Lily was scared and dropped the toy. It rolled under her bed and she could not see it anymore. She ran to her mom and told her what happened. Her mom hugged her and said it was okay. She said the toy was a remote control for the TV and it belonged to her dad. He must have left it on the floor by accident. Lily felt better and went back to her room. She took the remote control from under her bed and put it on her dad's desk. She did not want to play with it anymore. She liked to organize her toys, not make them noisy and scary.
Write a short story about Suddenly.

Once upon a time there was a girl who wanted to buy a cool bow. She reached into her pocket and realized that she had no money and was very sad. She tried to ask her mom for money, but her mom said no. The girl was so upset that she decided to run away. She ran and ran until she came to a big tree with a cool bow near the top. She was so excited! She wanted to reach it so badly. But when she tried to climb the tree, it was too tall, and she could not reach it. She wished there was something she could do to get the cool bow. Suddenly, she had an idea. She found a stick, put it into the tree, and used it to get the bow. She was so happy that she had finally reached her goal! Now she had the coolest bow in the world and she never had to worry about not having money again.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a brown bear. He was very patient and gentle, but he was all alone in the forest. He wanted to find a friend to play with but no one wanted to accept him. One day, the bear heard a noise coming from a nearby cave. When he looked inside he was surprised to find another brown bear! The two bears smiled at each other with joy and after that, they were best friends. The bears had lots of fun playing together and even shared their food. They even helped each other whenever there was a problem. The new bear was very grateful for the other bear's kindness and soon accepted him as part of their forest family. The patient brown bears were happy that they finally found a friend and together they lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Just.

One day, a small, harmless bug went for a walk. The bug saw a big ball of wool. The bug thought, "This wool looks fun to play with!" So, the bug went up to the wool and pinched it with its tiny legs. The wool rolled and rolled, and the bug laughed and laughed. Just then, a big cat came up to the bug and the wool. The cat said, "Hey, that's my wool! You can't play with it!" The bug felt sad and said, "I'm sorry, I didn't know it was yours." The cat looked at the bug and thought, "This bug is harmless and just wants to have fun." The cat had an idea. "Why don't we play with the wool together?" The bug smiled and said, "That sounds like fun!" So, the cat and the bug played with the wool all day long. They rolled it, pinched it, and laughed together. And, from that day on, the cat and the bug became the best of friends.
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily loves trains. She has a toy train that she plays with every day. She makes it go fast and slow, and makes noises like choo-choo and beep-beep. She wants to see a real train, but she has never been on one. One day, her mom says they can take the bus to the train station. Lily is very happy. She puts on her coat and hat, and holds her toy train in her hand. She says bye-bye to her dad and her cat, and goes outside with her mom. They wait for the bus at the stop. Lily is impatient. She asks her mom, "When will the bus come? When will we see the train?" Her mom says, "Soon, Lily, soon. Be patient." Lily tries to be patient, but she is bored. She looks at the cars and the trees and the birds, but she only wants to see the train. The bus finally comes. Lily and her mom get on the bus and sit down. Lily looks out the window, hoping to see the train. She sees more cars and trees and birds, but no train. She asks her mom again, "When will we see the train?" Her mom says, "Soon, Lily, soon. We are almost there." They get off the bus at the train station. Lily hears a loud noise. She looks up and sees a big, shiny train. It is red and blue and yellow, and it has a lot of windows and doors. It makes a loud choo-choo sound, and smoke comes out of its top. Lily is amazed. She says, "Wow, mom, look! It's a train! A real train!" Her mom smiles and says, "Yes, Lily, it's a train. Do you want to go inside?" Lily nods and says, "Yes, yes, yes!" She runs to the train with her mom, holding her toy train in her hand. She is very happy. She can't wait to see what the train is like inside.
Write a short story about Mia.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Mia. Mia was a very flexible girl. She could bend and twist in many ways. One day, Mia was playing in her room with her toys. Her room had a lot of furniture like a bed, a chair, and a table. Mia decided to play a game with her toys. She wanted to see if they could behave like her. She put her toys on the furniture and told them to bend and twist like her. But the toys did not move. Mia became sad and started to cry. Her mom heard her cry and came to see what was wrong. Mia told her mom about the game and how the toys did not behave. Her mom smiled and said, "Mia, toys are not like us. They cannot be flexible like you. But you can teach them to behave in other ways, like sharing and being kind." Mia learned that everyone is different, and we should be kind and patient with others.
Write a short story about Every.

Once upon a time, there was a big, popular tree in the park. Every day, many kids would play and have fun under the tree. The tree liked to see the kids happy. One day, a little bird came to the tree. The bird looked sad and said, "I worry that I am not good at flying. I see other birds flying high, but I can't do it." The tree wanted to help the bird. The tree told the bird, "You can practice flying from my branches. Start low and go higher as you get better. Don't worry, I will be here to help you." The bird was happy and started to practice flying. Soon, the bird became very good at flying and had lots of fun with the other birds. The tree was happy too.
Write a short story about Lily.

One day, a charming cat named Lily saw her image in a big shiny thing. She was scared. She thought it was another cat. Lily wanted to be the only cat in the house. Lily tried to pinch the other cat, but her paw just touched the shiny thing. It was cold and hard. She didn't understand why she couldn't pinch the cat. Lily's mom saw her trying to pinch the other cat. She told Lily, "That's not a real cat. That's just your image in the shiny thing." Lily felt silly, but she was happy there was no other cat. Now, Lily knew she was the only charming cat in the house.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there lived a bald man named Tom. He was very sad because he lost his hat. He looked everywhere but could not find it. Tom started to cry. One day, while walking, Tom saw a little cell. He heard a voice from inside. "Hello, can you help me?" the voice said. Tom was scared at first, but he wanted to help. Tom opened the cell and found a little bird. The bird was also bald and sad. "Thank you!" said the bird. They both smiled and became friends. Tom and the bird looked for a hat together. They found two hats, one for each of them. They were both happy and did not cry anymore.
Write a short story about Pippin.

Once upon a time, there was a little pony named Pippin. Pippin lived in a big, green field with his friends. Pippin loved to run and play all day. But one day, Pippin felt tired. He did not want to play anymore. Pippin's friend, a wise old owl, saw that Pippin was tired. The owl said, "Pippin, if you want to feel better, you should increase your rest. It is important to rest when you are tired." Pippin listened to the wise owl and decided to rest under a big tree. After resting, Pippin felt much better. He was not tired anymore. Pippin learned that it is important to rest when you are tired. Now, Pippin knows to increase his rest when he feels tired, so he can play with his friends every day. The moral of the story is to listen to your body and rest when you need to.
Write a short story about Soon.

Once upon a time, in a big garden, there was a little flower. The flower wanted to bloom and become big and pretty. The flower waited and waited for the sun to make it grow. One day, a boy came to the garden. He had some salt in his hand. He looked guilty because he knew he should not be there. The boy put the salt on the flower, thinking it would help it bloom. But the salt did not help the flower. The flower felt sad and did not bloom. A nice girl saw the flower and knew what to do. She gave the flower water and love. Soon, the flower bloomed and was big and pretty. The boy said sorry and the flower forgave him. They all became friends and played in the garden together.
Write a short story about Sue.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sue. Sue wanted to mail a pretty picture to her grandma. She needed glue to stick a note on the picture. She was grateful for her grandma and wanted to make her happy. Sue found the glue, but it was very old. She tried to open it, but it was stuck. Sue pulled and pulled, and then, something unexpected happened. The glue opened, but it went all over the picture. Sue was sad because her pretty picture was now all messy. But then, Sue had an idea. She took her messy picture and made a new picture with the glue. She made a big heart and wrote "I love you" in the middle. Sue was happy, and she knew her grandma would love it too. So, Sue mailed the new picture, and her grandma was very grateful for the special gift.
Write a short story about Bobo.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Bobo. Bobo lived in a cage. Every day, he would eat yummy seeds and drink water. Bobo was happy but he wanted to learn something new. One sunny day, the cage door opened. Bobo saw his chance to learn and flew out of the cage. He flew high and low, learning about the big world outside. Bobo saw many things like trees, flowers, and other animals. Bobo met a big, round creature. The creature said it was a cookie. Bobo thought cookies were yummy, so he was excited. But then, the cookie started to move! It wasn't a cookie, it was a turtle! Bobo laughed and learned that not everything is what it seems.
Write a short story about Whiskers.

Once upon a time, there was a prince who lived in a big castle. He had many friends, but his best friend was a little cat named Whiskers. They played together all day long. One day, the prince and Whiskers were playing hide and seek. The prince hid behind a tree, and Whiskers tried to find him. But when Whiskers found the prince, he saw that the prince had vanished! Whiskers was scared and called out, "Prince, where are you?" The prince heard Whiskers and called back, "I am here, Whiskers! I am safe!" Whiskers followed the sound of the prince's voice and found him hiding behind a big rock. They were both happy to see each other. Whiskers and the prince played together for the rest of the day. They knew they would always be safe and happy as long as they were together. And they lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, in a wild land far away, there lived a kangaroo. This kangaroo liked to jump and play all day. He had many friends who liked to play with him too. One day, the kangaroo had to choose a game to play. He could pick the safe game or the fun, wild game. The safe game was easy, but the wild game was more fun. The kangaroo wanted to have fun, so he picked the wild game. The kangaroo and his friends started to play the wild game. They jumped very high and very fast. But, they did not see the big hole in the ground. The kangaroo and his friends fell into the hole. They could not get out. The kangaroo was sad because he chose the wild game.
Write a short story about Sam.

Sam and Mia liked to play near the creek. They liked to splash in the water and look for fish and frogs. One day, they saw a big stick on the ground. Sam had an idea. "Let's make a bridge with the stick!" he said. "We can put it over the creek and walk on it." "OK!" Mia said. She helped Sam lift the stick and carry it to the creek. They tried to put it across the water, but it was too short. It fell into the creek with a splash. "Oh no!" Sam said. He was frustrated. He wanted to make a bridge. He tried to get the stick out of the water, but it was too heavy. He pulled and pulled, but it did not move. "Sam, don't be sad," Mia said. She saw that Sam was frustrated. She wanted to make him happy. She had an idea. She took Sam's hand and said, "Let's hold hands and jump over the creek. It will be fun!" Sam looked at Mia. He saw that she was smiling. He smiled too. He liked Mia. He liked to hold her hand. He said, "OK! Let's jump over the creek!" They held hands and ran to the edge of the creek. They jumped over the water and landed on the other side. They laughed and hugged. They were happy. They did not need a bridge. They had each other.
Write a short story about Mimi.

One day, a cute little cat named Mimi was out on a walk. She saw her friend, a big dog named Bobo. Bobo was sad. Mimi asked, "Why are you sad, Bobo?" Bobo said, "I want to go to the march, but my mom said I have to leave soon. I don't want to leave. I want to stay and play." Mimi thought for a moment. She wanted to help her friend. Mimi had an idea. She said, "Let's play a quick game before you leave. That way, you can have fun and still go to the march." Bobo smiled. They played a fun game of catch. Soon, it was time for Bobo to leave for the march. He was happy now, thanks to Mimi.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a big house. In the house, there was a rare, pretty bird. The bird lived by a window. The bird liked to look out the window and see the sun and the trees. One day, a mean cat saw the bird by the window. The cat wanted to catch the bird. The bird was scared. The bird needed help to protect itself from the mean cat. Then, something unexpected happened. A small dog saw the mean cat near the window. The small dog wanted to help the bird. The dog barked at the mean cat. The mean cat got scared and ran away. The bird was safe and happy. The small dog protected the bird, and they became good friends.
Write a short story about Every.

Once upon a time, there was a popular forest. Every day, lots of people served in the forest. They picked fruit, hunted for animals, and did many other things. One day, some people decided that they wanted to serve in the forest a different way. They started cutting down all of the trees in the forest. Soon, the whole forest was nothing but barren land. This made everyone very sad. It also caused a lot of damage to the animals who lived there. Without any trees, they didn’t have any places to hide or get food. This taught us an important lesson - we should always take good care of our forests! Serving in the forest should mean preserving it, not destroying it.
Write a short story about Tom.

One day, Tom went to the grocery store with his mom. They needed to buy food for dinner. Tom saw a thick, big cake and asked his mom, "Can we get the cake, please?" His mom said, "Not today, Tom. We need to buy healthy food." Tom was sad, but he helped his mom pick apples, carrots, and bread. When they finished shopping, they went home to cook dinner. In the kitchen, Tom saw a surprise. It was a thick paper with colors. His mom said, "I got this for you, Tom. You can fold it and make a fun toy." Tom was happy. He folded the thick paper into a cool airplane. He played with it all day and forgot about the cake.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a new wallet on the ground. He picked it up and saw it was red and shiny. Tim was very happy and ran to show his mom. "Mom! Look what I found!" Tim said, holding up the wallet. "Can I keep it?" His mom looked at the wallet and said, "We should try to find who it belongs to, Tim. Let's go ask our neighbors." Tim and his mom went outside and started to walk. As they walked, they saw a big car zoom by. It was so fast that it made Tim laugh. They asked their neighbors if they lost a wallet, but no one knew who it belonged to. Finally, they found an old man who said, "That's my wallet! Thank you for finding it." Tim gave the wallet to the old man, who was very happy. As a thank you, the old man gave Tim a toy car that could zoom really fast. Tim was happy that he helped someone and got a new toy to play with.
Write a short story about Jimmy.

It was a bright, sunny day and Jimmy was excited to go for a ride in his red fire engine. He loved pretending to be a brave fireman, rushing to put out fires and save the day. He liked pushing the pedal to make it go, and the wiggling the steering wheel this way and that. "Wheeee!" he shouted as he drove around. But then he saw a tire that was stuck in a muddy puddle. He got off and tried to pull it out, but it wouldn't budge. Jimmy was confused. Suddenly, he heard a voice behind him. He turned around and saw a real fireman, standing beside a big red truck. "Hey kiddo!" the fireman said, "Let me help you out with that." He bent down and with one tug, the tire was free. Jimmy was so excited he jumped up and down. He thanked the fireman, and gave him a big hug before getting back onto his own fire truck. Jimmy waved as he drove away, feeling proud to be a ride-along fireman.
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily and Ben were twins who liked to play in the kitchen. They had a toy stove, a toy fridge, and many toy dishes and spoons. One day, they decided to make some pretend soup with water and salt. They poured some water into a pot and added a pinch of salt. Then they looked for a teaspoon to stir the soup. But they could not find any teaspoon. They only had forks and knives and big spoons. They asked their mom, who was reading a book in the living room. "Mom, can we have a teaspoon, please?" Lily said. "Sorry, sweeties, we don't have any teaspoons left. They are all in the dishwasher," mom said. "But we need a teaspoon to make soup!" Ben said. "Why don't you use a big spoon instead?" mom suggested. "No, that's too big. It won't fit in the pot. And it will make too much noise," Lily said. "Well, I'm sorry, but you'll have to wait until the dishwasher is done. Or you can play something else," mom said. Lily and Ben were very upset. They wanted to make soup, not something else. They looked at each other and had an idea. "Let's go to the store and buy a teaspoon!" Ben whispered. "OK, let's go!" Lily whispered back. They put on their shoes and coats and grabbed their piggy banks. They sneaked out of the house and walked to the corner store. They saw many things in the store: candy, toys, books, and more. But they only wanted a teaspoon. They found one that was shiny and small and perfect for soup. They took it to the cashier and paid with their coins. "Thank you, come again!" the cashier said. Lily and Ben were very happy. They ran back to their house with their new teaspoon. They stirred their soup and tasted it. It was delicious. They shared it with their mom, who was surprised and proud. "Where did you get this teaspoon?" mom asked. "We bought it at the store!" Lily and Ben said. "You did? That's very clever of you. But next time, please ask me before you go out, OK? It's not safe to go alone," mom said. "OK, mom. We're sorry. We just really wanted a teaspoon," Lily and Ben said. Mom hugged them and smiled. "I understand. You are very creative and adventurous. But you are also very young. And I love you very much," mom said. Lily and Ben hugged her back and smiled. "We love you too, mom. And we love our teaspoon!" they said.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a toy horn that she loved to play with. She would blow the horn and make loud noises that made her laugh. One hot day, Lily went outside to play with her horn. She was having so much fun that she forgot to drink water and rest. That night, Lily had a dream. In her dream, she saw a wise old owl. The owl told her, "Lily, you must remember to take care of yourself when you play. Drink water and rest when it's hot." Lily listened to the wise owl's words and felt grateful for his advice. The next day, Lily went outside to play with her horn again. This time, she remembered to drink water and take breaks in the shade. She played all day long and felt happy and healthy. Lily learned that taking care of herself was important, and she would never forget the wise owl's words from her dream.
Write a short story about Spot.

Once upon a time, there was a helpful dog named Spot. Spot liked to try new things. One day, Spot's family gave him a big steak to eat. Spot had never eaten steak before. He was very happy to try it. Spot tried to eat the steak, but it was too big. He thought, "How can I eat this big steak?" Spot had an idea. He would ask his friend, Bird, for help. Bird was good at helping. Spot went to Bird and asked for help. Bird said, "I can help you, Spot. We can cut the steak into small pieces." Bird and Spot cut the steak into small pieces. Now, Spot could eat the steak. Spot and Bird were very happy. They had a fun day trying new things and helping each other.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a boy named Tim. Tim liked to enjoy his space. He liked to play with his toys in the big room. There was a lot of space available for him to play. One day, Tim's sister, Sue, came into the room. She wanted to play too. But Tim did not want to share his space. He said, "No, Sue! This is my space!" Sue was sad and cried. Mom heard Sue cry and came into the room. She said, "Tim, you need to share your space with your sister. There is enough space available for both of you to play." Tim thought about it and said, "Okay, Sue. You can play with me." Sue smiled, and they both enjoyed playing in the big room together.
Write a short story about Sally.

Once upon a time, there was a girl named Sally. She was walking through a meadow when she saw a hungry bee. The bee had gathered some pollen and wanted a lemon to sweeten it. Sally had a lemon in her pocket, but she felt bad for the bee. She didn't want to give it away. The bee started to urge her to give it the lemon, but Sally wasn't sure. She wanted the lemon for herself. She was feeling sleepy, so she decided to take a nap and think about what she should do. While Sally was sleeping, the bee got closer and closer. It kept urging her to give it the lemon. Suddenly, Sally woke up when the bee started to buzz in her ear. She quickly grabbed the lemon and threw it at the bee. The bee flew away with the lemon, happy that it got what it wanted. Sally was glad that she had been able to help the bee, even though she was a bit sleepy.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little gray bird named Tim. Tim was sad because he did something he didn't want to do. He felt regret. He was not happy. Tim wanted to make things better. One day, Tim heard some news. A big party was happening in the park. Tim thought, "I can go to the party and make new friends. That will help me feel better." So, Tim flew to the park to join the party. At the party, Tim met other birds who were happy to play with him. They sang songs and played games. Tim started to feel better. He learned that even when you feel regret, you can make things better by trying new things and making new friends. And Tim was never sad again.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to watch the world outside his window. Every day, he would see birds, cars, and people walk by. One day, Tim saw a rich man with a big hat. The rich man was giving out toys to all the kids. Tim ran outside and got a toy too. He was so happy and played with his new toy all day long. As the sun went down, Tim looked out his window again. He watched the world become dark and quiet. Tim smiled, hugged his toy, and went to bed. He knew he would watch the world again tomorrow.
Write a short story about Lily.

One day, a little girl named Lily found a small pebble. It was the most special pebble she had ever seen. She wanted to give it to her friend, Tom. She looked for something to wrap it in. Lily found a soft cloth and wrapped the pebble like a gift. Lily went to Tom's house, excited to give him the gift. She said, "I have a surprise for you, Tom!" Tom was very happy and wanted to see the surprise. Lily told him, "Wait until we go to the park. I will show you there." At the park, Lily and Tom found a nice spot under a big tree. Lily said, "Now, I will show you the surprise!" She took out the wrapped pebble and gave it to Tom. Tom opened it and saw the special pebble. He said, "Wow, thank you, Lily! This is the best gift ever!" They played with the pebble all day, and it became their favorite toy. From that day on, Lily and Tom were the best of friends. They always remembered how a simple gift brought them closer together.
Write a short story about Who.

Once upon a time, there was a eager deer. He loved to explore the forest and see what kind of new adventures he could find. One day, the deer was walking around and he spotted a little cabin. He wanted desperately to explore the cabin, but he was very shy. The deer crept up to the cabin and heard a voice inside. "Who's there?" it said. The deer was so startled he didn't know what to do. He heard the voice again, "Come on in, I won't hurt you!" The deer was a bit scared but eager to explore, so he opened the door and stepped inside. Inside the cabin, he saw a small family of people. They looked so friendly! They introduced themselves and showed the deer around. The deer loved exploring the cabin and meeting the family. They asked the deer if he wanted to stay, and the deer was so excited he quickly agreed! The deer said goodbye to the family and promised to come back soon. One day, a little girl named Sue was playing with her toy blocks. She wanted to make a tower with a big number of blocks. Sue's friend, Tom, came over to help her build the tower. Sue thought that was a great idea, so they started to build the tower again. As the tower got taller, it became harder to reach the top. Suddenly, a little bird flew into the room and landed on the tower. The tower started to shake and wobble. Sue and Tom both shrugged, not knowing what to do. The bird looked at them and said, "I can help you make the tower taller!" The little bird picked up the small blocks with its beak and put them on top of the tower. With the bird's help, Sue and Tom made the tallest tower they had ever seen. They were so happy and thanked the little bird for its help. The bird smiled and flew away, leaving Sue and Tom with their amazing tower.

Tim and Sam were brothers who liked to play pirates. They had a big box that they pretended was their ship. They put some toys and books inside the box and called them their treasure. They wanted to sail to a new island and find more treasure. One day, they saw a dark cave near their house. They thought it might have some hidden treasure inside. They decided to load their box with their treasure and drag it to the cave. They put on some hats and scarves and said, "Arr, mateys, let's go explore the cave!" They reached the cave and pushed their box inside. It was very dark and cold. They could not see anything. They heard some strange noises and felt some wet things on the floor. They were scared. They wanted to go back to their house. They tried to pull their box out of the cave, but it was too heavy. They had loaded it with too much treasure. They could not move it. They cried for help, but no one heard them. They were stuck in the cave with their treasure. They wished they had not been so greedy. They wished they had stayed at home and played with their toys and books. They were very sad and sorry.

One day, a girl named Lucy found a flute. She was glad because she loved music. She took the flute and went to the river to bathe. The big fish jumped out of the water and tried to take the flute from the frog. The frog and the fish fought, but the fish was stronger. The fish took the flute and swam away. Lucy and the frog were sad because they lost the flute and could not make music anymore.

They all had a fun day at the park.

Once there was a seed. The seed sat in the ground, alone in the dirt. The sun shone down on the seed, and it began to grow. Up it grew until it became a tall celery plant. The celery kept growing and growing, making everyone happy!

She smiled and said, "I know what will open this lock" and reached out for a small tool. She used the tool to carefully push a button on the lock and it made a CLICK. The little girl was so excited and said, "Oh, it opened! What do we have inside?” Inside the lock were two bags of candy! The little girl ate a few pieces, but the mommy said, "No, don't spoil yourself. Save some of the candy for later, okay?” The little girl hugged her mommy and said, "Of course!" She put the candy back inside the lock and said, "Oooh, this is very special". And from that day on, the little girl and her mommy saved the candy inside the delicate lock, so the little girl could have a special treat every once in a while. The end.

Once upon a time there was a little boy. His name was Alex, and he was three years old. Alex needed things like food and water. He also needed a hug and a kiss. But most of all, he needed his mother. Once upon a time, there was a big bull named Tom. Tom lived on a farm with many other animals. One day, a new truck came to the farm. The truck brought a new bull named Tim. Write a short story about Bob.

Once upon a time, there was a cheerful fish named Bob. Bob liked to pick up little stones from the sea floor. One day, he saw a big, dark shadow in the water. Bob did not know what it was, but he felt scared. Bob swam fast to his friend, Sally the sea turtle. He told her about the big shadow. Sally knew that it was a shark. She told Bob to be careful and stay near the rocks, so the shark could not find him. Bob listened to Sally and hid in the rocks. Later, the shark swam close to the rocks. He was looking for a fish to eat. But Bob was safe in his hiding spot. The shark could not see him. Bob was so happy that he had listened to Sally. From that day on, Bob and Sally were the best of friends. They played together and picked up stones, always staying close to the rocks.

Cat said, "Okay, Mimi. But I want some cheese too." Mimi agreed, and they both started to move the cheese together. But then, Dog saw them and got angry. "Stop! That cheese is mine!" he yelled. Mimi, Cat, and Dog all wanted the cheese, but they didn't know how to share. They started to argue about who should get the cheese. In the end, they decided to share the cheese together. They all became friends and enjoyed the yummy cheese.

Anna likes to play with her mom's makeup. She thinks it is fun to put colors on her face and pretend she is a princess. But she knows she has to be careful and not make a mess. One day, when her mom is busy, Anna sneaks into her bedroom and finds her makeup bag. She opens it and sees many shiny things. She takes out a huge lipstick and tries to put it on her lips. But it is too big and she smears it all over her mouth and chin. She looks in the mirror and laughs. She wants to try more things, so she takes out a blush and a mascara. She rubs the blush on her cheeks and nose, making them very pink. She opens the mascara and tries to put it on her eyelashes, but it is too hard and she pokes her eye. She cries out loud. Her mom hears her and comes into the bedroom. She sees Anna with makeup all over her face and the bed. She is shocked and angry. "Anna, what have you done?" she asks. "This is not a toy, this is my makeup. You are not allowed to touch it. You have made a huge mess. You have to wash it off right now." Anna feels sorry and scared. She does not want her mom to be mad at her. She says, "I'm sorry, mom. I just wanted to play. I wanted to be like you. You are so pretty." Her mom sighs and hugs her. She says, "I love you, Anna. But you have to ask me before you use my things. And you don't need makeup to be pretty. You are beautiful just the way you are. Now, let's go to the bathroom and wash your face. Suddenly, a bee came out of the flower. It buzzed and flew around Lily's head. Lily was scared. She screamed and ran away. Ben laughed. He thought it was funny. He said, "I told you the flower was fake. The bee was fake too. It was a toy. I put it there to trick you. It was a joke." Lily was angry. She did not like Ben's joke. She said, "That was not a joke. That was mean. You made me feel bad. You are not a good friend." Ben felt sorry. He realized he was wrong. He said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I did not mean to hurt you. I was just having fun. Please forgive me. I will not do it again." Lily looked at Ben. She saw he was sincere. She said, "OK, Ben. I forgive you. But next time, be nice. Do not play tricks on me. Or on anyone else." Ben nodded. He said, "You are right, Lily. I will be nice. I will not play tricks. I will be a good friend." Lily smiled. She said, "Thank you, Ben. You are a good friend. Do you want to play with me?" Ben smiled too. He said, "Yes, Lily. I want to play with you. What do you want to play?" Lily said, "How about we play with the slide? It is fun and real." Ben agreed. He said, "OK, let's play with the slide. It is fun and real. And no more fake flowers or bees." They held hands and ran to the slide. They played and laughed. They were happy. The moral of the story is: Do not play tricks on your friends. It is not nice. It can hurt their feelings. Be honest and kind. That is how you make good friends.
Write a short story about Bob.

One day, a smart rabbit named Bob was playing in the woods. Bob loved to spring high into the air and feel the wind on his face. He would jump and jump, laughing and having fun. Suddenly, there was a big shake. It was an earthquake! Bob was scared and didn't know what to do. He saw a big tree and decided to hide under it until the shaking stopped. After a while, the earthquake was over. Bob came out from under the tree and saw that his friends were okay too. They all knew that being smart and finding a safe place to hide had saved them. From that day on, Bob and his friends always knew what to do when the ground shook, and they continued to play and spring in the woods, happy and safe.
Write a short story about Tom.

Once upon a time, in a small town, there was a mechanic named Tom. He was a very nice man who would fix cars. One day, Tom was feeling anxious because he had to fix a big truck, and he didn't know how to do it. Tom's friend, Sally, came to visit him. She saw that Tom was feeling anxious and asked, "Tom, why are you so anxious?" Tom said, "I need to fix this big truck, but I don't know how to use the tools." Sally wanted to help her friend. She said, "Don't worry, Tom, I can show you how to use the tools!" Tom was happy that Sally was there to help him. They worked together and fixed the big truck. After they were done, Tom was no longer anxious. He thanked Sally for her help and they went to play in the park. From that day on, Tom was never anxious again because he knew he could always ask his friends for help.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom and Sam are friends. They like to play with cars. They have many cars of different colors and sizes. Tom has a big red car that is very fast. Sam has a small blue car that is very shiny. One day, they decide to have a race with their cars. They find a long road near their house and draw a line with a stick. They put their cars behind the line and get ready to start. "Are you ready, Sam?" Tom asks. "Yes, I am ready, Tom," Sam answers. "OK, let's go! One, two, three, go!" Tom shouts. They push their cars as hard as they can. The cars zoom along the road. Tom's car is faster than Sam's car. It reaches the end of the road first. Tom is happy. He jumps and cheers. "I win, I win!" Tom says. Sam is sad. He walks to the end of the road and picks up his car. He looks at Tom's car and sighs. "Your car is better than mine, Tom. It is big and fast. My car is small and slow. You are wealthy. I am poor," Sam says. Tom hears Sam and feels sorry for him. He likes Sam and does not want him to be sad. He thinks for a moment and then smiles. "Sam, do you want to play another game?" Tom asks. "What game, Tom?" Sam asks. "Let's play match. We can match our cars by color. You have a blue car and I have a red car. We can find other cars that are blue and red and put them together. It will be fun," Tom says. Sam thinks and then nods. "OK, Tom, let's play match. That sounds fun," Sam says. Tom and Sam run to their house and look for other cars that are blue and red. They find many cars and match them by color. They make a big pile of blue cars and a big pile of red cars. They laugh and have fun. They forget about the race and the wealthy and the poor. They are just friends who like to play with cars.
Write a short story about Ben.

Once upon a time, there was a big bear named Ben. Ben loved to play in the woods. One day, he saw a clear sign. The sign said, "Be kind to your friends." This sign reminded Ben to be nice to everyone. Ben had a friend named Tim, the tiny mouse. Tim was sad because he lost his toy. Ben wanted to help. He looked everywhere in the woods. Ben found the toy and gave it back to Tim. Tim was so happy and said, "Thank you, Ben!" From that day on, Ben always remembered the sign. He was kind to all his friends in the woods. Everyone loved Ben, and they all played happily together. The moral of the story is to always be kind to your friends.
Write a short story about Audrey.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Audrey. Audrey was only three years old and loved to explore the world around her. One day Audrey went for a walk in the park with her mom. Audrey's mom asked her a question: "Where do you want to go?". Audrey thought for a moment, and then said, "Let's go to the pond." The two of them passed many trees and flowers until they finally reached the pond. Audrey was so excited to see the pond, but when they got close the water looked uncomfortable and scary. Audrey's mom said, "Don't worry Audrey, you don't have to go in the pond if you don't want to." Audrey smiled and said, "I'm okay, let's just look at it." The two of them watched the pond together, and Audrey's mom passed her a few questions about the animals living in the pond. Audrey answered them all with a smile on her face. The two of them slowly walked away from the pond. Audrey was happy to have seen the pond, even if it made her feel a bit uncomfortable. And they lived happily ever after.
Write a short story about Tim.

One day, a little boy named Tim found a flea in his room. He looked at the flea and said, "Hi, flea! Do you want to play with me?" The flea jumped up and down, happy to play with Tim. Tim and the flea played with a ball. The ball was heavy, but they had fun. They threw the ball to each other and laughed. Tim said, "Flea, can you imagine we can be best friends?" The flea jumped high, showing it was happy to be friends with Tim. At the end of the day, Tim and the flea were tired. They lay down on the soft bed and closed their eyes. The flea was very small, but it was a big friend to Tim. They knew they would play together again tomorrow.
Write a short story about No.

Once upon a time, in a peaceful little town, there lived a cat and a dog. They were good friends and liked to play together. But one day, they had a quarrel over dinner. The cat said, "I want to eat fish for dinner tonight." The dog disagreed and said, "No, I want to eat chicken tonight." They could not agree on what to have for dinner, and they started to argue. Finally, the cat and the dog decided to ask their friend, the wise old owl, for help. The owl listened to their problem and said, "Why don't you both have dinner together? The cat can have fish, and the dog can have chicken." The cat and the dog liked the idea and decided to have dinner together. They stopped quarreling and became friends again. From that day on, they always enjoyed their dinner together, and the peaceful town was happy once more.
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, in a small house, lived a girl named Lucy. She loved to mix things together. One gloomy day, she found some mud outside. She thought it would be fun to play with it. So, she took some mud inside her house. Lucy mixed the mud with water in a big bowl. She felt the texture of the mud. It was soft and squishy. She liked how it felt in her hands. Lucy thought it would be fun to make something with the mud. Lucy made a little mud house with her hands. She put it by the window to dry. When the sun came out, the mud house became hard. Lucy was happy with her little mud house. She showed it to her mom and dad. They were proud of her and her fun creation.
Write a short story about Jack.

Once upon a time there were two friends, Jack and John. They were always playing together and they would joke around all the time. One day, they were walking by a pond and Jack saw a huge net in the water. He was very curious to know what was inside of it. Jack asked John to help him get the net out of the water. John thought Jack was joking and they both started laughing. But Jack was serious and he kept insisting that they needed to get the net. Eventually, John agreed and they both went into the water to get the net. When they finally got it out of the water, they saw that it was full of fish! Jack and John were so surprised, they had no idea what to do with all the fish! They looked at each other and started to joke again, then they both agreed to take the fish home to their families. The two friends had a great time as they carried the net full of fish back home. Jack was very curious to know what their families would think when they saw all of the fish. He knew it was going to be a funny surprise!
Write a short story about Bob.

Once upon a time, there were two best friends: Bob and Tim. They were always together and never fought, until one day when they had to accept a new job. They both wanted to work in the same factory. "No, I want to work in the factory," said Bob. "I want to work in the factory," said Tim. "We can't both work in the same factory," said Bob. "No, I have to have this job!" said Tim. Bob was getting a little bossy and said, "It's my turn, I want this job!" But Tim didn't accept it and started to argue. Just then, the boss came and said, "Stop arguing. You can both have jobs in the factory!" Bob and Tim smiled and hugged each other in excitement. They both accepted the job and ran off to start their first day together.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She had a favorite patch on her dress. The patch was red and had a picture of a cat. Lily loved her patch very much. One day, Lily's friend Sam came to play. Sam saw the patch on Lily's dress and said, "I don't understand why you like that patch so much. It looks spoiled." Lily felt sad, but she tried to explain. "I like my patch because it is special to me." Sam thought for a moment and then said, "I'm sorry, Lily. I didn't know it was special to you. I understand now." Lily smiled and they played together all day, happy to be friends who understand each other.
Write a short story about Tim.

Tim and Lily were playing in the garden. They saw a big vine that grew on the wall. It had many green leaves and purple flowers. "Let's climb the vine!" Tim said. "It looks like fun!" Lily was not sure. "But what if we fall? Or what if Mom and Dad get angry?" Tim shrugged. "Don't be scared. They won't see us. And the vine is tough. It can hold us." He grabbed the vine and started to pull himself up. Lily followed him, but she felt nervous. The vine was high and wobbly. They reached the top of the wall and looked around. They saw the neighbor's dog, a big and black one. It barked at them and ran towards the wall. "Uh-oh!" Tim said. "The dog is mad at us! We have to go down!" They tried to slide down the vine, but it was not easy. The vine was rough and scratchy. It hurt their hands and legs. The dog jumped and snapped at them. "Help!" Lily cried. "Mom! Dad!" Mom and Dad heard them and came outside. They saw Tim and Lily on the vine and the dog on the ground. They were shocked and worried. "Tim! Lily! What are you doing?" Mom shouted. "Get down from there!" Dad grabbed a stick and scared the dog away. He helped Tim and Lily down from the vine. They were dirty and bleeding. Mom hugged them and checked their wounds. She was angry and sad. "Why did you climb the vine?" she asked. "You could have been hurt badly! Or bitten by the dog! You know you are not allowed to do that!" Tim and Lily looked down. They felt sorry and ashamed. "We wanted to have fun," Tim said. "We thought the vine was tough." "But it was not safe," Lily said. "We were wrong." Mom and Dad took them inside and cleaned their wounds. They gave them a hug and a kiss. "We love you, Tim and Lily," they said. "But you have to be careful and listen to us. We only want you to be safe and happy." Tim and Lily nodded. They learned their lesson. They never climbed the vine again.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Sue. Sue loved her mom's toast. Every morning, her mom made toast for breakfast. Sue felt proud when she could help her mom in the kitchen. One day, Sue's friend Tim came to play. Tim was hungry, so Sue wanted to make toast for him. Sue asked her mom if she could help. Her mom said yes and showed Sue how to make the toast. Sue tried her best and soon the toast was ready. Tim was happy when the toast arrived. He said it was the best toast he ever had. Sue felt very proud of herself. She learned that helping others and trying her best can make her feel good. And that was the moral of the story: always do your best and help others when you can.
Write a short story about Hi.

Once upon a time, there was a flexible kite. It loved to fly high in the sky. The kite was very happy when it was up in the air. One day, the kite met a bird. The bird was also flying in the sky. "Hi, bird!" said the kite. "I like to fly in the sky. Do you like it too?" "Yes, kite!" said the bird. "I love to fly in the sky. It is so much fun!" One day, the bird saw a little girl. The girl was sad because she had no toys. The bird wanted to give her a gift. The bird thought the flexible kite would make her happy. "Kite, will you let me give you to the little girl? She is sad and needs a toy," asked the bird. "Yes, bird! I will make her happy," said the kite. So, the bird gave the flexible kite to the little girl. The girl was so happy to have a new toy. She played with the kite every day, and they both loved to fly high in the sky together.

One day, a green bird lived in a cage. The cage was in a big tree. The bird was sad in the cage. It wanted to fly and play with other birds. The bird did not like the cage. A boy saw the bird in the cage. He wanted to help the bird. The boy opened the cage. The bird flew out of the cage. The bird was happy. It flew up high in the sky. But then, something strange happened. The bird started to rot. It fell from the sky. The boy was sad. He did not know why the bird rotted. The bird was not a real bird. It was a toy bird. The boy did not know. He thought it was a real bird.

One day a brave driver was driving down a road. He was so brave and strong, he thought nothing could ever hurt him. Suddenly he saw an animal in the road and he knew he had to make the decision – to stop or keep going. He tried to think of the best thing to do and then he decided to keep going. The animal ran away in time, but then the driver heard a terrible noise. He had hit a rock and the car had turned over. The driver was hurt and scared, but he was also very brave. He tried to make the car work again, but he couldn't do it. He had to wait there until help arrived. The brave driver was safe but his car was broken and he was sad.
Write a short story about Amy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Amy. She had a dirty spade that she loved to play with in her yard. One sunny day, Amy went outside to dig in the dirt with her spade. As she dug, she saw a big cat. The cat was sleepy and let out a big yawn. Amy said, "Hi, cat! Do you want to play with me and my spade?" The cat looked at Amy and said, "Yes, I want to play!" So, Amy and the cat played in the dirt with the dirty spade. They dug and dug until they were both very tired. They both yawned and went inside to rest. Amy and the cat became best friends and played together every day.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a big green crocodile. He lived in a pond near a small village. The crocodile was very restless. He would swim and play all day long. One day, the crocodile found a broken toy boat near the pond. He wanted to help, so he decided to repair the boat. The crocodile used some sticks and leaves to fix the boat. He worked very hard until the boat was like new again. The next day, a little boy from the village came to the pond. He saw the crocodile and the fixed toy boat. The little boy was so happy to see his boat was fixed. He thanked the crocodile and they became good friends. They played with the boat in the pond every day, and the crocodile was not restless anymore.
Write a short story about No.

One day, a thin cat and a big dog were playing in the park. The cat was very fast and the dog was very strong. They were both very excited to play together. But then, they saw a ball with the word "power" on it. They both wanted to play with the ball. The cat said, "I found it first, so it's mine." The dog said, "No, I saw it first, so it's mine." They both tried to grab the ball, but the cat was too thin and the dog was too strong. The dog got the ball and ran away. The cat was very sad and went home. In the end, the thin cat and the big dog did not play together anymore. They both lost a friend because they fought over the ball with the word "power" on it. It was a bad ending for both of them.
Write a short story about Every.

Once upon a time there was a big, colorful ground. It was filled with sun, trees, and grass. Every day lots of animals came to the ground. They came to play and have fun. But, something strange also happened. At night when the animals were asleep, a mysterious figure appeared on the ground. He was a tall man wearing a white coat. He was very gentle and he would walk around the ground, taking care of everything. The creatures didn't quite understand why the man kept coming around. But they were thankful to have him. He carefully looked over their trees and flowers and gave them the care they needed. One night, when the man came around, he had a big surprise. He had brought along a giant cupcake. He wanted to share it with the creatures, so he carefully divided it between them. Everyone enjoyed the delicious treat. The animals finally understood why the man kept coming around. He was there to serve them and take care of them. From that day forward, the mysterious figure became known as the Servant of the Ground. He served that colorful ground until the end of time!
Write a short story about Max.

One day, a big dog named Max went for a walk. Max liked to walk slow in the fog. The fog was soft and cool. Max saw a little cat named Lily. Lily was sitting on a big rock. Max said, "Hi, Lily! Do you want to walk with me in the fog?" Lily said, "Yes, Max! I like the fog too." So Max and Lily walked slow in the fog. They were happy. Max and Lily found a big stick. Max liked to chew on sticks. Max said, "Look, Lily! A big stick to chew!" Max chewed the stick and Lily watched. They played in the fog until it was time to go home.
Write a short story about Millie.

Millie had a new square. It was a bright yellow square with buttons on it. It was a remote control! Millie loved it and she knew it belonged to her. Millie used the remote control to turn on the TV and watch her favourite show. She could use it to make the TV louder, or to change the channel. Whenever Millie was not using the remote control, she kept it in a drawer in her room. It belonged to her, and she wanted to keep it safe. Millie loved her new square. It made her feel so grown up. She wanted to show it to everyone, but she knew it belonged only to her.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, in a big garden, there was a little girl named Lily. She was very independent and liked to do things by herself. One day, Lily saw a tree with small, round fruits on it. She did not know what they were, so she asked her mom, "Mommy, what are these?" Her mom replied, "Those are prunes, Lily. They are good for you, but you need to ask for help when picking them." Lily wanted to pick the prunes by herself, but she remembered her mom's words. She waited for her mom to come and help her. They picked the prunes together and had a fun time. Later, Lily's friend, Tim, came to play. He saw the prunes and wanted to pick them too. But he did not ask for help. He tried to climb the tree and fell down. He got a big boo-boo and cried. Lily learned that it is good to be independent, but sometimes it is better to ask for help. This made her a wise and happy girl.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. She was excited because today was the festival. Lily wanted to learn how to make a beautiful kite for the festival. She asked her mom to help her. Lily and her mom started to make the kite. But it was hard for Lily. She tried and tried but the kite did not look good. Lily felt frustrated. Her mom said, "Don't worry, we can try again." They tried again and again. Each time, the kite looked better. Finally, Lily made a beautiful kite. She was so happy. At the festival, Lily and her mom flew the kite together. They had so much fun. And Lily learned that trying again can help when you feel frustrated.
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily and Ben are friends. They like to play shop. Lily is the shopkeeper and Ben is the customer. Lily has a lot of things to sell. She has beef, bread, milk, eggs, and cheese. Ben has some money to buy. Ben comes to Lily's shop. He says hello to Lily. He looks at the things. He wants to buy some beef. He asks Lily, "How much is the beef?" Lily says, "The beef is five coins." Ben says, "That is too much. Can you make it cheaper?" Lily says, "No, I cannot. The beef is good and fresh. It is not old or bad." Ben thinks for a while. He really wants the beef. He says, "Okay, I will buy the beef. But can you give me something else too?" Lily says, "What do you want?" Ben says, "I want a hat. It is cold outside and my head is cold." Lily says, "I have a hat. But it is very tight. It might not fit you." Ben says, "Let me try it." He puts the hat on his head. It is very tight. It hurts his head. He says, "Ouch! This hat is too tight. I do not want it." Lily says, "I told you it was tight. Do you want something else?" Ben says, "No, I do not. I just want the beef. Here are five coins." He gives Lily the money. Lily gives him the beef. She says, "Thank you for buying from my shop. Come again soon." Ben says, "You are welcome. Thank you for selling me the beef. Goodbye." He takes the beef and goes home. He is happy. He will cook the beef and eat it with his family. Lily is happy too. She has more money for her shop. She hopes more customers will come and buy her things.
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, there was a small house near the woods. In this house, there lived a happy girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to play outside with her toy car and her big, round ball. One day, Lucy saw a boy near her house. The boy looked sad and had a frown on his face. Lucy wanted to make the boy happy, so she asked him to play with her. The boy's name was Tom. They played together with the toy car and the big, round ball. The sky was clear and the sun was shining. As they played, Tom's frown turned into a big smile. Lucy and Tom became good friends and played together every day near the small house.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to play with his toys all day. One day, Tim's uncle came to visit. His uncle was very nice and liked to play with Tim too. Tim and his uncle were playing with Tim's toy cars. Tim had a red car and his uncle had a blue car. They raced their cars across the empty floor, making "vroom vroom" sounds. Suddenly, Tim's car crashed into his uncle's car. Tim's uncle said, "Oh no, Tim! We need to be careful with our toys." Tim felt sad and said, "I'm sorry, uncle." His uncle smiled and said, "It's okay, Tim. Just remember to be careful next time. I won't punish you, but let's clean up the mess we made." So Tim and his uncle picked up the cars and put them back in their places. After they cleaned up, Tim and his uncle played more games together. Tim was very happy to have his uncle visit and they had lots of fun. Tim learned to be more careful with his toys, and he knew his uncle was always there to help him.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a great big tree. In the tree, there was a small nest. A dove lived in the nest. The dove had a friend, a small boy named Tim. Tim and the dove liked to play together. One day, Tim saw the dove in the tree. He said, "Dove, come down and play with me!" The dove wanted to play, but it was scared. Tim urged, "Please, dove, do not be scared. I will catch you if you fall." The dove trusted Tim, so it jumped from the tree. Tim caught the dove in his hands. They played all day and had lots of fun. The dove was happy it had a friend like Tim. And Tim was happy too because he had a great friend in the dove.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom liked to whistle. He whistled when he walked, when he played, and when he was happy. He learned to whistle from his dad, who whistled a lot too. Tom wanted to whistle like his dad, who could make different sounds and tunes with his mouth. One day, Tom and his dad went to the park. They saw a big, modern building with glass and metal. Tom thought it looked shiny and cool. He asked his dad what it was. "That's a museum, Tom. It's a place where people can see old and new things, like paintings and statues and machines. Do you want to go inside and look?" his dad said. "Yes, please!" Tom said. He was curious and excited. They went inside the museum and saw many things. Tom liked the colorful paintings and the funny statues. He also liked the machines that moved and made noises. He whistled along with some of them. His dad smiled and whistled with him. They came to a room that had a sign that said "Rocks and Minerals". Tom saw many stones of different shapes and colors. Some were shiny, some were dull, some were smooth, some were rough. He wanted to touch them and feel them. "Can I touch the stones, dad?" he asked. "Sure, but be careful. Some of them are very old and fragile. And don't take any of them with you, okay? They belong to the museum," his dad said. "Okay, dad," Tom said. He touched some of the stones gently. He liked how they felt in his hand. He saw one stone that was round and white. It had a hole in the middle. It looked like a donut. He thought it was funny and cute. He picked it up and put it in his pocket. He forgot what his dad said. They left the museum and went back to the park. Tom and his dad played catch with a ball. Tom was happy and whistled. He felt the stone in his pocket. He wanted to show it to his dad. "Dad, look what I found in the museum!" he said. He took out the stone and showed it to his dad. "Tom, that's not yours. That's a fossil. It's a very old stone that has the shape of an animal that lived a long time ago. It's very rare and valuable. You can't take it from the museum. That's stealing. You have to give it back," his dad said. He was angry and disappointed. Tom felt bad. He didn't know it was a fossil. He didn't mean to steal. He liked the stone, but he didn't want to make his dad mad. He wanted to do the right thing. "I'm sorry, dad. I didn't know. I'll give it back. Please don't be mad at me," he said. He started to cry. His dad hugged him and said, "It's okay, Tom. I'm not mad at you. I'm glad you told me the truth. You made a mistake, but you can fix it. Let's go back to the museum and return the fossil. Maybe they'll let you whistle with the machines again." Tom nodded and smiled. He wiped his tears and whistled. He held his dad's hand and walked back to the museum. He hoped they would forgive him. He learned a lesson. He would never take anything that wasn't his again. He would only whistle.
Write a short story about Can.

Once upon a time, there was a little panda who loved to swing. One sunny day, the panda went to the park. The park was very busy with many kids playing. The little panda saw a big swing and wanted to play on it. He asked a girl, "Can I swing too?" The girl said, "Yes, you can swing after me." The panda waited and watched the girl swing high. The girl and others came to help him, but it was too late. The little panda could not play anymore.

John and Mom watched the frog for a few minutes. The frog kept hopping around the porch. John: "What does the frog do?" Mom: "It hops around and looks for food. Write a short story about John.

Once upon a time there were two brothers. One brother was called John and the other one was called Joe. John owned a reliable car and Joe owned a toy tooth. John and Joe got in a fight because Joe wanted to use John's car. John told Joe that he could not have the car because he owns a toy tooth. Joe was sad and did not understand why he could not have the car. He asked John why he could not have the car. John explained that he could not give him the car because it is important to be reliable and dependable. He said that he only wanted to lend his car to people who are reliable. Joe thought about this for awhile. He realized that it is important to be dependable and reliable so that when others need your help, you can give it to them. Joe decided to put away his toy tooth and work on gaining John's trust by being reliable and dependable. From that day forth, Joe and John became the best of friends. The moral of the story is that it is important to be reliable and dependable in order to be trusted by others.
Write a short story about Lily.

Lily was a curious girl who liked to explore new things. One day, she saw a big crane near her house. It was lifting heavy things and making loud noises. Lily wanted to see it closer, so she ran to the fence and climbed on it. "Hey, what are you doing there?" a man shouted from the crane. He was wearing a yellow hat and a blue shirt. He looked angry. "I want to see the crane," Lily said. "It is big and cool." "You can't be here, it is dangerous," the man said. "The crane can hurt you, or you can fall. And there is fire over there, it can burn you. Go back to your house, now!" Lily saw the fire. It was orange and red, and it was making smoke. It was coming from a pile of wood and metal. Lily felt scared. She did not like the fire. It was hot and bad. "OK, I will go," Lily said. She climbed down from the fence and ran to her house. She did not want to see the crane anymore. She wanted to be safe and warm. She told her mom what she saw. Her mom hugged her and said, "I'm glad you are OK, Lily. You should never go near the crane or the fire. They are not for playing. They are for working. And you should always listen to the man in the yellow hat. He knows what is best." Lily nodded. She learned her lesson. She was still curious, but she knew some things were better to watch from far away. She decided to play with her toys instead. She liked her toys. They were fun and nice.
Write a short story about Lucy.

Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lucy. Lucy loved to create things with her hands. She would make drawings, paint pictures, and fold paper into fun shapes. One day, Lucy decided to make a special folder for her art. Lucy found a big, broken box in her room. She thought it would be perfect to make her folder. She cut the box into a flat piece and folded it in half. Then, she used colorful tape to fix the broken parts and make it pretty. Lucy was so happy with her new folder. She put all her drawings, paintings, and paper shapes inside. Every day, Lucy would create more art and add it to her folder. She was proud of her art and loved to show her folder to her friends and family.
Write a short story about Tim.

Once upon a time, there was a little boy named Tim. Tim loved to eat prunes. One day, he found an interesting prune tree in his yard. The tree was big and had many prunes. Tim wanted to get the prunes from the tree. He asked his friend, Sam, "Can you help me get the prunes?" Sam said, "Yes, let's do it!" They tried to reach the prunes, but they were too high. So, Tim and Sam found a big stick to poke the prunes. As they poked the prunes, some fell on the ground. Tim and Sam were happy. But then, Tim slipped on a prune! He fell and laughed. Sam laughed too. They picked up the prunes and shared them. They had a fun day with the interesting prune tree.
Write a short story about Dare.

Once upon a time, there was a little bird named Dare. Dare lived in a big tree near a river. He had many friends who played with him every day. One day, Dare found a small pebble near the river. He thought it was very useful. He decided to show it to his friends. Dare picked up the pebble with his beak and flew to his friends. Dare showed the pebble to his friends, but they did not think it was useful. They all laughed at Dare. This made Dare very sad. He flew away with the pebble and did not play with his friends again.
Write a short story about Janet.

Janet had a special surprise for the school's assembly. She was excited to show it off! "What do you have?" asked her friend Amy. "A magic wand," Janet said. The next day, she pointed her wand into the air and said, "Let's make something yummy for the assembly tomorrow!" The week went by quickly as Janet made something special for the assembly. Everyone was excited to find out what her surprise was. At the assembly, she pulled out a big box that had a yummy smell coming from it. Everyone clapped in excitement. Janet opened the box and pulled out the most delicious chocolate cake anyone had ever seen. The assembly erupted in cheers and everyone enjoyed the yummy cake! Janet knew that her magical wand had made the assembly extra special.
Write a short story about Lily.

Once upon a time, in a big field, there was a small flower. The flower wanted to bloom and be strong like the big trees. One day, a kind girl named Lily came to the field. She saw the small flower and said, "You can be strong too!" Lily took the small flower to a temple. The temple was a special place where flowers could grow big and strong. Lily said to the flower, "You will bloom here and become strong like the trees." The small flower was happy and excited. Days went by, and the small flower started to grow. One day, Lily came back to the temple. She saw the flower had bloomed and become strong. "Look at you!", she said. "You are so big and strong now!" The flower was very happy and thanked Lily for helping it grow.
Write a short story about Tom.

Tom and Mia are friends. They like to play with blocks and dolls. But one day, Tom is angry. He does not want to share his blocks with Mia. He says, "Go away, Mia. These are my blocks. You are bad." Mia is sad. She does not understand why Tom is angry. She likes Tom and his blocks. She says, "Please, Tom. Can I play with you? I am not bad. I am your friend." Tom does not listen. He pushes Mia away. Mia falls down and hurts her knee. She cries. She sees blood on her knee. She is scared. Tom feels sorry. He did not mean to hurt Mia. He says, "I am sorry, Mia. I did not want to hurt you. I was angry. I hate this note." Mia looks at the note. It is from Tom's mom. It says, "Tom, you have to go to the dentist today. I know you do not like it, but it is good for your teeth. I love you." Mia says, "Oh, Tom. I understand. You are afraid of the dentist. I am afraid too. But it is not so bad. The dentist is nice. He gives you a sticker and a toy. And your mom loves you. She wants you to be healthy." Tom smiles. He says, "Thank you, Mia. You are a good friend. I am sorry I was mean to you. Can I give you a hug?" Mia nods. She says, "Yes, Tom. I forgive you. Can we play with the blocks and the dolls together?" Tom says, "Yes, Mia. We can. And can you help me write a note to my mom? I want to tell her I love her too." Mia says, "Of course, Tom. I can help you. Let's write the note with crayons. And let's draw a heart and a smiley face." Tom and Mia hug. They are happy. They write the note. They play with the blocks and the dolls. They are friends.
Write a short story about upon.

Once upon a time, there was a big tank. It was a fierce tank, but it was also very nice. The tank lived in a land where everything was hot. One day, the tank met a new friend. The friend was a small ice cube. The ice cube was very cold. The tank and the ice cube liked to play together. They played fun games like hide and seek, and tag. But the ice cube started to feel too hot. It was not happy in the hot land. The ice cube began to melt. The tank saw the ice cube melting and wanted to help. It thought of a plan. The tank took the ice cube to a cold place, where it would not melt. The ice cube was happy again. The tank and the ice cube stayed friends forever, and they played together whenever they could.
Write a short story about Now.

Once upon a time, there was a huge leopard. He had big spots and lived in a forest. The huge leopard loved to play with his friends. They would run, jump, and laugh all day long. One day, the huge leopard found a big tree. He wanted to climb it. He tried and tried, but he could not get up. He felt sad. His friends saw him and wanted to help. They had a big idea. They got a long vine and tied it to the tree. Then, they pulled the vine tight. The huge leopard held on to the vine and his friends pulled him up. They all laughed and cheered as the huge leopard reached the top of the tree. Now, the huge leopard could see the whole forest. He was so happy and had lots of fun with his friends.
Write a short story about Hello.

One day, a little red ant was walking in the grass. The sun was shining, and the ant was happy. He saw a big tree and wanted to climb it. As he started to climb, he saw a big cat. "Hello, cat," said the ant. "Can you help me climb the tree?" The cat looked at the ant and said, "I can help you, but I need something first." The cat had to sneeze. "Achoo!" The cat sneezed so hard that the little red ant flew up into the tree. "Thank you, cat!" said the ant. He was now in the big tree. The ant was very happy and played in the tree all day. The cat and the ant became good friends and played together every day.
Write a short story about Mia.

Once upon a time, a little girl named Mia went to the park. She had a big red bag with her. In the bag, she had a ball, a toy car, and a rare, shiny rock. Mia loved her shiny rock because it was very special. At the park, Mia saw a boy named Tom. Tom wanted to learn how to throw a ball. Mia said, "I can help you!" So, they took the ball out of the bag and played together. Mia showed Tom how to throw the ball, and soon he learned. After they played, Mia showed Tom her toy car and the rare shiny rock from her bag. Tom liked the shiny rock too. They became good friends and promised to play together again. They put everything back in the bag and went home, happy to have learned and shared.
Write a short story about Lila.

Lila and Ben were playing in the park. They liked to run and slide and swing. But then Ben fell down and hurt his knee. He cried and cried. Lila ran to him and said, "Don't cry, Ben. I will help you." Lila took out her backpack and opened it. She had a bandage inside. She said, "Look, Ben. I have a bandage for you. It will make your knee feel better." She put the bandage on Ben's knee and said, "There. Now you are brave and strong." Ben stopped crying and smiled. He said, "Thank you, Lila. You are a good friend. Can I see your bandage?" Lila nodded and said, "Sure, Ben. But you have to be careful. It is a special bandage." Lila lifted her sleeve and showed Ben her arm. She had a bandage too. But under the bandage, there was something shiny and colorful. It was a sticker. Lila said, "This is my sticker. It is a star. I got it from the doctor. He said I was brave too." Ben gasped and said, "Wow, Lila. Your sticker is so pretty. Can I have one too?" Lila said, "Maybe, Ben. But you have to go to the doctor first. He will check your knee and make sure it is not bad. Then he will give you a sticker too." Ben said, "Okay, Lila. Let's go to the doctor. Maybe he will have a sticker for me too. But can I keep your bandage? It is so soft and dry."
